headline: Ofwat undertakings
brief: South East Water settles Ofwat investigation with £30.5m redress package replacing initial £22.5m fine.
summary: South East Water concluded Ofwat's investigation into supply interruptions and licence breaches with a £30.5 million legally binding redress package. The commitment replaces an initially proposed £22.5 million fine and funds resilience improvements (£13m), smart metering acceleration (£5m), water conservation initiatives (£5m), business storage and metering (£5m), critical infrastructure protection (£1m), and community relief (£1.5m), aligned with the company's PR24 investment programme.
retail_take: Market likely views this neutrally-to-positively: higher cost than initially proposed, but customer-focused remediation and regulatory closure reduce ongoing uncertainty and reputational risk.

headline: Trekor Announces 36 Million Pounds of Copper Production from Gibraltar and Florence Copper in the Second Quarter
Ruvuma Operations and Corporate Update
brief: Aminex's Ruvuma operator requests significant 2026 work programme cuts, delaying first gas production and Chikumbi-1 drilling.
summary: ARA Petroleum Tanzania Limited (APT), operator of the Ruvuma PSA and Ntorya Development, has requested material amendments to reduce the approved 2026 work programme and budget following management changes and technical reappraisal. The proposed cuts will delay first gas production and the Chikumbi-1 well drilling. Aminex and Tanzania Petroleum Development Corporation (TPDC) have not approved these amendments. Discussions are ongoing among Aminex, APT, Zubair Corporation, and TPDC to reach agreement. Aminex reserves contractual remedies including recourse to Zubair's parent company guarantee.
retail_take: Negative read likely; project delays and operator disputes raise execution risk and push cash generation timelines further into future, creating uncertainty for investors seeking near-term value.

headline: Luansobe Licence Update
brief: Galileo Resources' Luansobe copper project licences disappeared from Zambian mining register; legal challenge underway.
summary: Galileo Resources' two Small-Scale Mining Licences (34543/HQ/SML and 34545/HQ/SML) for the Luansobe Copper Project in Zambia no longer appear on the Zambian Mining Cadastre portal. Local partner Statunga Investments Limited, which holds the licences, filed a formal complaint on 12 June 2026 with the Minerals Regulation Commission and has launched court proceedings challenging the cancellation filed 30 June 2026. Galileo holds a 75% interest through a JV agreement dated 30 December 2021. An injunction has been obtained against third party Mopani Copper Mines. Advanced partnership discussions have been suspended pending resolution.
retail_take: Material negative: project development halted, 75% stake at risk, and significant uncertainty over legal outcome in Zambian courts creates substantial downside for equity investors.

headline: Admission to Trading
brief: Babcock International admits £250m bond notes to LSE main market trading.
summary: Babcock International Group PLC has admitted £250 million of 5.125% Notes due 2031 to trading on the London Stock Exchange main market on 14 July 2026. The Notes (ISIN: XS3437585022) were issued under the company's £3 billion Euro Medium Term Note Programme. Full details are contained in the base prospectus dated 3 March 2026 and final terms dated 10 July 2026, available on the LSE website and the FCA's National Storage Mechanism.
retail_take: Routine debt capital market transaction completing issuance mechanics; signals Babcock managing refinancing needs and liquidity profile; likely neutral to slightly positive as successful execution de-risks funding.

headline: Annual Financial Report
brief: Albion Enterprise VCT reports modest 1.46% NAV growth for year to March 2026; fund size £292.3m.
summary: NAV per share fell to 112.19p from 116.22p despite positive portfolio returns of £7.1m, as dividends (5.73p) exceeded gains. Fund disposed of £18.1m investments including partial Oviva sale at 5.1x return. Largest holdings: Quantexa (£54.1m, 18.5% NAV), Oviva and Proveca (combined 31% NAV). New 2.80p dividend declared for August 2026. VCT legislation changes effective April 2026 reduce upfront income tax relief from 30% to 20%; company campaigning for reversal.
retail_take: Muted performance reflecting portfolio volatility amid AI sector uncertainty, though underlying asset quality seen as defensive. Tax relief reduction materially impacts VCT attractiveness to new investors; existing holders face no immediate impact.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: BlackRock's holding in Hill & Smith crosses 5% threshold, reaching 5.08% of voting rights.
summary: BlackRock, Inc. notified Hill & Smith PLC on 13 July 2026 that it crossed the 5% voting rights threshold on 10 July 2026. BlackRock now holds 3,910,439 direct voting rights (4.98%) plus 79,115 voting rights through securities lending agreements (0.10%), totalling 5.08% and 3,989,554 voting rights. The previous notification showed holdings below 5%. The notification was filed under TR-1 requirements following an acquisition of voting rights.
retail_take: Routine major shareholding disclosure; BlackRock's 5%+ stake signals institutional confidence but is typical for a major asset manager and unlikely to materially move markets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares - Replacement
brief: JSG purchased 2.01m shares (168.8p-172.1p range) in weekly buyback; intends cancellation.
summary: Johnson Service Group repurchased 2,006,925 ordinary shares between 6-10 July 2026 via Investec Bank at weighted average prices ranging from 147.37p to 169.50p daily. Total spending approximately £3.4m. On 10 July, share price fell sharply to 136.1p-169.9p range, reflecting broader market weakness. Shares will be cancelled, reducing share count and potentially accretive to EPS. Programme announced 7 May 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution; buyback at declining prices (especially 10 July) may signal weak momentum. Neutral-to-slightly-negative: supports EPS but timing coincides with share price weakness.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Oxy Capital increases stake in Franchise Brands to 5.01% of voting rights.
summary: Oxy Capital - SGOIC, S.A., a Portuguese entity controlled by Miguel Calle da Cunha Lucas, crossed the 5% notifiable threshold in Franchise Brands PLC on 14 July 2026. The holding comprises 9,706,032 direct voting rights representing 5.008684% of total voting rights, up from 4.995782% in the previous notification. No financial instruments are held. The disclosure was made on the same date in London.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a notifiable holding increase. The minimal movement (0.013%) suggests incremental accumulation rather than a strategic change, likely signalling consolidation by an existing investor.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: British Land's AGM passes all 21 resolutions with strong shareholder support across governance, dividend, and director appointments.
summary: All resolutions passed at British Land's 14 July 2026 AGM with overwhelming majorities. Key approvals include: Annual Report (99.88% for), final dividend of 10.80p per share (99.99% for), re-appointment of all directors including new director Raj Shah (99.97% for), PwC as auditor (90.87% for), and share allotment authorities. Board changes include appointing Raj Shah as director. Share buyback authority and general meeting notice period (89.29% for) also approved. Voting participation was 74% of issued capital.
retail_take: Routine AGM approval with no contentious votes; strong support across all governance matters indicates shareholder confidence. Non-event for markets.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Société Générale increases stake in International Personal Finance to 6.97% from 5.89%.
summary: Société Générale, registered in London, crossed a major shareholding threshold in International Personal Finance Plc on 10 July 2026, increasing its voting rights from 5.893016% to 6.967442% (15.51m shares). The notification was made to the issuer on 13 July 2026. No financial instruments were involved in the acquisition.
retail_take: Routine major holdings notification; suggests continued confidence in IPF by institutional investor but typically a non-event unless triggering governance concerns or indicating strategic repositioning.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Debenhams Group AGM: Nine ordinary resolutions passed, two special resolutions on pre-emption rights failed.
summary: All nine ordinary resolutions passed at the 14 July 2026 AGM with 61-99% support, including annual accounts approval (61.32%), director re-elections (62-63%), auditor re-appointment, and share buyback authorization (99.86%). Two special resolutions on disapplication of pre-emption rights failed to achieve requisite majorities. Voter turnout represented 73.74-73.77% of issued share capital.
retail_take: Failed pre-emption rights resolutions signal shareholder resistance to potential dilutive financing flexibility, though core governance and buyback approval secure. Mixed signals on capital management suggest investor caution on future funding options.

headline: Granting Of Share Awards
brief: LondonMetric grants nil-cost LTIP share options to executives following new remuneration policy approval.
summary: Following AGM approval of its new remuneration policy on 9 July 2026, LondonMetric Property PLC granted further nil-cost options on 10 July 2026 under its Long Term Incentive Plan for the year ended 31 March 2026. CEO Andrew Jones received 342,249 options and Martin McGann received 179,680 options. Vesting is subject to total shareholder return, total accounting return, and earnings performance conditions measured over three years.
retail_take: Routine executive remuneration disclosure showing alignment of management incentives with shareholder returns through performance-conditional awards; no material market implications.

headline: ESTIMATED GROWTH  FOR THE FIRST HALF OF 2026
brief: Huatai Securities expects H1 2026 net profit of RMB11.3-11.7bn, up 50-55% YoY.
summary: Huatai Securities forecasts H1 2026 net profit attributable to parent shareholders of RMB11.324-11.702 billion, representing growth of RMB3.775-4.153 billion or 50-55% versus H1 2025's RMB7.549 billion. Excluding non-recurring items, net profit expected at RMB11.333-11.706 billion (up 52-57% YoY). Growth driven by strong capital market activity and revenue increases across wealth management, institutional services, investment management and international business segments. Figures are preliminary and unaudited.
retail_take: Strong profit growth signals robust capital market conditions and effective execution of diversification strategy, likely supporting the stock. However, figures are estimates pending audit and actual results may vary.

headline: Final Terms
brief: Xtrackers ETC PLC issues 60,000 Physical Gold ETC Securities (Tranche 657) under its precious metals programme.
summary: Xtrackers ETC PLC has issued 60,000 units of its Xtrackers IE Physical Gold ETC Securities due 2080 at USD 54.42 per security, generating estimated proceeds of USD 3.26m. The securities are denominated in USD with a metal entitlement of 0.0154 fine troy ounces per unit and a 0.11% base fee. Gold holdings are custodied by JPMorgan Chase on an allocated basis. Total series outstanding increases to 97.7m securities with maximum issuance capacity of 100bn units.
retail_take: Routine product issuance within existing ETC programme; represents minor tranche addition. Demonstrates continued investor demand for physically-backed gold exposure but carries commodity price risk and counterparty credit exposure to JPMorgan.

headline: TR-1: Notification of major holdings
brief: Dale Smith-Kind notifies of 3.96% stake in Tower Resources PLC, crossing disclosure threshold.
summary: Dale Smith-Kind has notified the FCA of holding 3.96% of voting rights in Tower Resources PLC, representing 1,594,013,487 shares (ISIN: GB00BZ6D6J81). The threshold was crossed on 24 June 2026, with notification to the issuer and regulator occurring on 14 July 2026. The holding comprises direct voting rights only, with no financial instruments involved.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholding crossing the 3% notification threshold. No indication of strategic intent or market-moving developments provided.

headline: Exercise and Lapse of Equity Incentive Securities
brief: Greatland Resources employees exercise 437,500 options and 247,329 performance rights; 63,998 unvested rights lapse.
summary: Greatland Resources announced that non-PDMR employees exercised 437,500 vested options and 247,329 vested performance rights, each convertible to one ordinary share satisfied through transfers from the employee share trust with no new shares issued. Separately, 63,998 unvested performance rights issued to former employees lapsed following their employment cessation. The exercises and lapses are routine equity incentive plan activity.
retail_take: Routine administrative disclosure of employee equity plan activity with no capital dilution; likely a non-event for market participants as shares were transferred rather than newly issued.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Savills CEO James Sparrow acquires 16 shares via employee share plan at £9.04 per share on 10 July 2026.
summary: James Sparrow, CEO of Savills UK & EMEA and PDMR, purchased 16 ordinary shares through the Savills Share Incentive Plan on 10 July 2026 at £9.04 per share. Following this allocation, Sparrow holds 6,466 shares in the Plan and maintains a total beneficial interest of 170,935 Savills ordinary shares. This routine monthly share purchase plan transaction was notified to the market in accordance with EU Market Abuse Regulation requirements.
retail_take: Routine PDMR share plan participation disclosure; insider purchasing is generally viewed neutrally in the absence of broader market context, representing standard employee benefit participation.

headline: JPMC&Co. 8-K 2Q2026 earnings release 14 July 2026
brief: JPMorgan Chase files Q2 2026 earnings release as regulatory announcement on Euronext Dublin and UK FCA.
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. submitted its Form 8-K Current Report dated July 14, 2026, containing the earnings press release for the quarter ended June 30, 2026. The filing was submitted to Euronext Dublin and the UK Financial Conduct Authority's National Storage Mechanism, as required for regulatory compliance. The full document is available on the SEC website and JPMorgan Chase's investor relations portal.
retail_take: Routine regulatory filing; earnings details require access to the actual 8-K document for material substance. Market read depends on Q2 earnings performance itself, not this announcement.

headline: Amendment and Extension of Unsecured Loan Notes
Updates
brief: Axis Bank completes preferential equity issuance in subsidiary Axis Finance Limited to Kedaara investors.
summary: Axis Bank has completed the preferential issuance of equity shares in its subsidiary Axis Finance Limited (AFL) to Kedaara Pearl Holding and Kedaara Capital Fund IV AIF on July 13, 2026. Following the allotment, AFL is no longer wholly-owned by Axis Bank; the parent now holds 94.92% of AFL's paid-up equity share capital while maintaining its position as holding company and promoter. All conditions precedent and regulatory approvals were fulfilled prior to completion.
retail_take: Routine capital transaction completion in a subsidiary; dilution to 94.92% ownership is immaterial and reflects strategic capital raising rather than loss of control. Market likely treats as non-event.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: First Tin chairman's holding restructured via dividend in specie; no change to beneficial interests.
summary: Arlington Group Asset Management Limited transferred 56,655,961 ordinary shares (10.46% of issued capital) to Radoon Capital Limited (controlled by Charles Cannon Brookes, Non-Executive Chairman) and Simon Catt on 13 July 2026. Cannon Brookes now holds 28,327,981 shares (5.23%) via Radoon Capital; Catt holds 28,327,980 shares (5.23%). Both previously held 50% interests in Arlington Group. The transaction, effected as a dividend in specie, resulted in no change to ultimate beneficial ownership.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of director shareholding restructuring with no economic impact or change in control; likely a tax or administrative reorganisation with minimal market relevance.

company: CC Japan Income & Growth Trust PLC
headline: Monthly Factsheet as at 30 June 2026
brief: CC Japan Income & Growth Trust releases monthly factsheet for 30 June 2026.
summary: CC Japan Income & Growth Trust PLC published its monthly factsheet as at 30 June 2026 on 14 July 2026. The factsheet, available on the company's website, includes portfolio commentary from the Portfolio Manager. Managed by Frostrow Capital LLP, the trust is a UK-listed investment company regulated to be recommended by independent financial advisers to retail investors.
retail_take: Routine monthly disclosure providing investors with updated performance and portfolio data; no material news or market-moving information evident from the announcement itself.

headline: Annual Financial Report
brief: CTRL Section 1 Finance PLC files annual financial report for year ended 31 March 2026 with FCA.
summary: CTRL Section 1 Finance PLC, a company with listed debt, has submitted its annual financial report for the year ended 31 March 2026 to the National Storage Mechanism in compliance with Listing Rule 17.3.1 and Disclosure and Transparency Rules 6.2.10 and 4.4.1. The document is now available for inspection via the FCA's NSM platform. The linked document provided is general FCA guidance on NSM processes rather than the actual financial results.
retail_take: Routine regulatory filing; no substantive financial data disclosed in this announcement. Market impact likely negligible unless the underlying report reveals material developments—actual results require direct review of the filed accounts.

headline: TR-1: Notification of major holdings
brief: Richard Griffiths notified of 5.11% voting rights stake in Mpac Group PLC, crossing disclosure threshold.
summary: Richard Griffiths, based in Jersey, crossed a notifiable threshold in Mpac Group PLC on 10 July 2026, holding 5.11% total voting rights. This comprises 0.89% direct voting rights via 267,500 ordinary shares (ISIN GB0005991111) and 4.22% through CFDs (1,267,967 voting rights). The notification was submitted to the FCA on 13 July 2026. Previous position stood at 3.74%, indicating a 1.37 percentage point increase.
retail_take: Routine major shareholding notification showing an increase in stake through a combination of direct shares and derivatives; typical disclosure with no immediately apparent material implications for the business or strategy.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Four easyJet directors purchased Partnership Shares on 10 July 2026 at £6.74 per share through the employee share plan.
summary: Four persons discharging managerial responsibility at easyJet purchased Partnership Shares on 10 July 2026: CEO Kenton Jarvis, COO David Morgan, Chief Customer and Marketing Officer Robert Birge, and CEO easyJet holidays Garry Wilson. Each purchased 22 shares at £6.74 per share through Equiniti Share Plan Trustees Limited under the HM Revenue and Customs-approved Share Incentive Plan, funded by salary deductions capped at £150 monthly per participant.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of director participation in standard employee share plan. No material significance; demonstrates modest management alignment with minimal financial commitment and indicates confidence in ordinary circumstances.

headline: Statement regarding investment in Vaultz Capital
brief: Hamak Strategy backs Vaultz Capital's Bitcoin exit strategy, supporting shareholder vote on 21 July to withdraw Bitcoin treasury policy.
summary: Hamak Strategy, holding 6.5 million ordinary shares in Vaultz Capital (V3TC), will vote for resolutions to withdraw V3TC's Bitcoin treasury policy and grant authority to dispose Bitcoin holdings. V3TC's NAV was approximately £8.0 million (3.1p per share) with 134 Bitcoin as of June 2026. Hamak's board believes the resolutions provide flexibility to realise value and reposition V3TC as a cash-backed acquisition platform, potentially retaining value as a listed vehicle.
retail_take: Market likely reads this as positive governance signal: a major shareholder backing liquidity from illiquid Bitcoin holdings to enable active deal-making rather than passive holding. Suggests confidence in V3TC management execution despite crypto volatility.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Liontrust Investment Partners reduces stake in Alfa Financial Software from 9.14% to 4.997%.
summary: Liontrust Investment Partners LLP, controlled by Liontrust Asset Management Plc, crossed a threshold by reducing its voting rights holding in Alfa Financial Software Holdings PLC from 9.14% to 4.997% (14.92 million shares) on 13 July 2026. The notification was filed on 14 July 2026. No financial instruments were involved in the transaction. This represents a significant reduction in the investor's stake below the 5% reporting threshold.
retail_take: Routine disclosure; no read-through. Standard shareholding notification showing a major investor's partial exit, likely reflecting portfolio reallocation rather than fundamental concerns about the company.

headline: Notification of major holdings
brief: BGF Investment Management crosses 8.36% voting stake in Animalcare Group PLC via acquisition of voting rights.
summary: BGF Investment Management Limited, acting via BGF Investments LP and holding shares through Pershing Securities Limited, crossed the 8.362% voting rights threshold in Animalcare Group PLC on 13 July 2026. The stake comprises 5,773,946 ordinary shares representing 8.362% of voting rights, up from a previous position of 5.919%. The threshold crossing was notified to the FCA on 14 July 2026. BGF Group plc is the ultimate controlling entity.
retail_take: Routine major holdings disclosure showing increased institutional investment; likely signals confidence in the business but represents standard regulatory notification with no material implications for operations or shareholder value.

headline: Admission to Trading (XS3383869826, SPUK 118)

summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce has admitted GBP 250,000 Preference Share Linked Notes (ISIN: XS3383869826, ticker: SPUK 118) to trading on the London Stock Exchange's main market on 14 July 2026. The notes mature in July 2031 and are issued under CIBC's Structured Note Programme. Full terms are available via the base prospectus dated 23 January 2026 and final terms published 3 July 2026, available on LSE and the FCA's National Storage Mechanism.
retail_take: Routine capital markets activity; structured note admission indicates CIBC accessing GBP funding markets but represents standard product placement with no material strategic implications.

The document has been filed and will be available for inspection via the FCA's NSM portal. No financial metrics, performance data, or material substance details were disclosed in the announcement itself.
retail_take: Routine regulatory filing; no material information disclosed beyond confirmation of submission. Market impact likely neutral unless underlying report contains significant news when accessed.

headline: Admission to Trading (XS3373528390, SPUK 115)

summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce has admitted GBP 250,000 Preference Share Linked Notes due July 2031 (XS3383869826, SPUK 118) to trading on the London Stock Exchange main market on 14 July 2026. retail_take: Routine debt capital markets issuance expanding CIBC's funding sources; LSE admission is administrative completion with no immediate market implications for equity investors.

headline: Admission to Trading (XS3383376129, SPUK 117)
brief: CIBC admits GBP 250,000 preference share linked notes due 2031 to LSE trading.
summary: Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce announced admission to trading on 14 July 2026 of GBP 250,000 Preference Share Linked Notes due July 2031 (XS3383869826, SPUK 118) on the London Stock Exchange main market under its Structured Note Programme. Full details available in the base prospectus dated 23 January 2026 and final terms published 3 July 2026, accessible via LSE and FCA's National Storage Mechanism.
retail_take: Routine capital markets execution; structured note issuance is standard debt financing activity with no material implications for equity holders or broader market sentiment.

headline: No intention to make an offer
USFM Update
brief: 80 Mile notes JV partner USFM's proposed merger with Nasdaq-listed Twin Vee PowerCats, expects Q3-Q4 2026 close.
summary: 80 Mile's joint venture partner USFM Corporation announced a proposed merger with Nasdaq-listed Twin Vee PowerCats Co (VEEE), with transaction expected to close in Q3 or Q4 2026 and the combined entity trading on NYSE American. 80 Mile is not party to the transaction. The company expects to continue collaboration with USFM on the 2026 exploration drilling programme at the Disko-Nuussuaq Project, where USFM is funding US$30 million in drilling-related expenditure.
retail_take: Largely procedural update with no direct impact on 80 Mile; demonstrates continuity of partner commitment to Disko drilling programme, though USFM's merger adds corporate restructuring risk to the JV timeline.

headline: Ferguson Enterprises Inc. (“Company”): Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Four Ferguson directors purchased shares via dividend reinvestment on 8-9 July 2026 at $220-$224 per share.
summary: Directors Kelly Baker, Catherine Halligan, James S. Metcalf, and Chief Digital & Information Officer James Paisley purchased Ferguson common stock through independent dividend reinvestment arrangements between 8-9 July 2026. Baker acquired 5.47 shares at $220.62-$224.20; Halligan purchased 11.05 shares at $221.19-$224.20; Metcalf bought 24.74 shares at $222.60-$224.20; Paisley acquired 2.86 shares at $224.19. Transactions occurred on NYSE.
retail_take: Routine director share purchases via dividend reinvestment plans show management confidence in holding equity; typical corporate governance disclosure with no material strategic implications for investors.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: Sealand Capital Galaxy (LSE: SCGL) confirms all resolutions passed at 14 July 2026 AGM.
summary: Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited held its Annual General Meeting on 14 July 2026, with all shareholder resolutions duly passed. The Cayman Islands-incorporated technology investment company, listed on the London Stock Exchange's Main Market since November 2015, focuses on acquiring and developing technology, e-commerce and IT companies targeting Asia-Pacific expansion, with particular emphasis on AI-driven solutions for emerging Asian markets.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure confirming standard AGM approval; no material business updates, strategic changes or financial information disclosed to warrant market reaction.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: CEO Paul McNerney purchases 2,716 Severfield shares at 35.9p per share on 14 July 2026.
summary: Severfield PLC CEO Paul McNerney bought 2,716 ordinary shares at 35.9p each on 14 July 2026, totalling £975.04, via market purchase on the London Stock Exchange. This represents a routine director share transaction requiring regulatory disclosure under UK MAR rules.
retail_take: Modest CEO share purchase suggests confidence in the business but at a small scale relative to typical executive compensation; likely viewed as routine director trading with minimal market impact.

headline: Holding(s) in Company

summary: RiverFort Global Opportunities PCC Ltd crossed a voting rights threshold in Forgent PLC on 13 July 2026, with notification made 14 July 2026. retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major shareholding change; RiverFort's reduced stake below 3% threshold likely signals a partial exit with no material strategic implications for investors.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding

summary: Two M&S PDMRs acquired shares via the company's Dividend Reinvestment Plan (DRIP) on 10 July 2026. Both transactions were conducted on the London Stock Exchange Main Market.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of share purchases through dividend reinvestment; modest holdings increases indicate executive participation in company growth, though immaterial in scale.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: JMH completed a share repurchase of 25,000 ordinary shares on 14 July 2026 at prices ranging from US$61.1025 to US$61.81, with weighted average purchase price of US$61.3029. The repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, JMH's issued share capital comprises 294,158,976 ordinary shares with one vote each; the company holds no treasury shares.

summary: Next 15 Group plc is admitting 424,414 new ordinary shares (2.5p each) to AIM as consideration for vesting of long-term incentives related to M. Booth & Associates LLC. The shares rank pari passu with existing capital. Following issuance, the company will have 101,860,099 ordinary shares in issue with no treasury holdings. Admission expected by 16 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine equity issuance for incentive plan vesting; modest dilution (0.4%) with no material capital raise or strategic implications; non-event for investors.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: PayPoint repurchased 80,196 shares between 7-13 July 2026 at an average of 586p per share; shares will be cancelled.
summary: PayPoint plc purchased 80,196 ordinary shares via Investec Bank between 7-13 July 2026 across London Stock Exchange, with daily volume-weighted average prices ranging from 580.06p to 595.48p. Total spend approximately £4.7m. All shares will be cancelled. Share capital post-buyback: 59,790,230 shares. Individual trade-level detail provided per MAR Article 5(1)(b) requirements.
retail_take: Routine capital management signal; share buyback with cancellation reduces share count and typically supports EPS but is non-material corporate housekeeping. Market typically reads small buybacks as neutral unless part of larger strategic programme.

headline: Holding(s) in Company

summary: Barry Reynolds, based in Dudley, UK, crossed a disclosure threshold on 13 July 2026 by acquiring voting rights in Fragrant Prosperity Holdings Ltd (ISIN VGG368811037). retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholder threshold crossing; minimal market significance absent context on Reynolds' strategic intent or board representation.

summary: BlackRock, Inc. crossed a notifiable threshold in ConvaTec Group PLC (ISIN GB00BD3VFW73) on 10 July 2026, increasing its total voting rights position from 5.51% to 5.53%.
