company: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd
headline: 2026 Interim Performance Forecast
brief: Ming Yang Smart Energy issues interim forecast showing H1 2026 net profit of RMB 100-150m, down 75-84% YoY.
summary: Ming Yang Smart Energy expects H1 2026 net profit attributable to shareholders of RMB 100-150 million, a decline of RMB 459.9-509.9 million or 75.41-83.60% year-on-year versus RMB 609.9 million in H1 2025. Excluding non-recurring items, profit expected to be RMB 5 million to minus RMB 45 million, down 98.97-109.27% YoY from RMB 485.3 million. Decline driven by significantly lower power station product sales due to construction and sales progress issues, offset partly by increased overseas market investment.
retail_take: Significant profit deterioration signals operational challenges in core power station business, though expanded overseas activities suggest strategic repositioning. Market likely negative given 75%+ profit decline despite growth investments.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Jeremy Attard-Manche crosses 5% threshold with 6.76% stake in Ondo InsurTech PLC
summary: Jeremy Attard-Manche, resident in Malta, acquired voting rights in Ondo InsurTech PLC (ISIN GB00BNVVGD77) crossing a notifiable threshold on 10 July 2026. He now holds 16.67 million shares representing 6.76% of voting rights, with no additional exposure through financial instruments. The issuer was notified on 13 July 2026, three days after the threshold was crossed.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of major shareholding crossing 5% threshold. No material context provided on acquirer identity, acquisition rationale, or strategic intent; likely market non-event absent further announcements.

company: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd
headline: Issuance of 2026 Green Sci-Tech Bonds (Tranche 2)
brief: Ming Yang Smart Energy issues RMB 500m second tranche of 3-year green bonds at 1.8% coupon.
summary: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group successfully completed the second tranche of its 2026 Green Sci-Tech Innovation Bonds on July 14, 2026. The RMB 500 million issuance, priced at par with a 1.8% coupon and 3-year maturity (redemption July 2029), was fully subscribed. This tranche forms part of an approved RMB 3 billion medium-term note programme registered in April 2026, with China CITIC Bank as bookrunner and lead underwriter.
retail_take: Successful bond issuance demonstrates investor demand for the company's green financing and strong market access, but the low 1.8% coupon reflects current market conditions rather than company-specific strength.

headline: Tunnel Kiln Testwork - Clarification
brief: European Metals clarifies tunnel kiln cost savings disclosed 8 July were preliminary and not final forecasts.
summary: European Metals Holdings clarified that preliminary US$112m capex savings announced 8 July 2026 regarding tunnel kiln testwork lack detailed study backing and do not constitute updated financial forecasts under ASX Listing Rule 5.17. The company confirmed its December 2025 Definitive Feasibility Study remains current guidance. If adopting the tunnel kiln option and optimising the Lithium Chemical Plant flowsheet, EMH will update the DFS by end-2026 (indicative timeframe). Cinovec is Europe's largest hard rock lithium resource in Czech Republic.
retail_take: Restatement of preliminary nature suggests company faced regulatory scrutiny over overstated capex savings claims; likely perceived negatively as a walk-back despite remaining exploratory upside if optimisation materialises.

headline: Share Buyback Programme Update
brief: Volex cuts share buyback programme from £40m to £20m following $74.7m KST SignalTek acquisition.
summary: Volex PLC has reduced its on-market share buyback programme from £40 million to £20 million, announced today following the acquisition of remaining shares in KST SignalTek Ltd for $74.7 million initial cash consideration, taking ownership to 100%. The company stated the reduction demonstrates capital discipline while maintaining financial flexibility for growth investments. No other terms of the original April 2026 buyback programme were changed.
retail_take: Markets likely view this positively as disciplined capital allocation—prioritising strategic M&A over shareholder returns signals management confidence in acquisition returns and growth strategy execution.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Hongkong Land repurchases 190,000 shares at US$7.19-7.34 for cancellation.
summary: Hongkong Land Holdings repurchased 190,000 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at prices ranging from US$7.19 to US$7.34 per share, with a weighted average of US$7.2424. The shares will be cancelled. Post-repurchase, issued share capital comprises 2,135,400,826 ordinary shares with one vote each. The company holds no treasury shares.
retail_take: Modest share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management activity that could modestly support EPS but signals no major strategic shift or distress.

headline: Acquisition of remaining stake in Kepler SignalTek
brief: Volex acquires remaining 64.3% stake in Kepler SignalTek for up to $89.4m, taking ownership to 100%.
summary: Volex completes acquisition of remaining stake in Kepler SignalTek (KST) for $74.7m upfront plus up to $14.7m deferred consideration tied to revenue targets. KST, a specialist manufacturer of patient-connected medical products, generated $51.8m revenue in FY2026 with 10% organic growth. Volex held 35.7% prior to acquisition. Deal expected immediately earnings-accretive with EBIT margins exceeding group average, targeting 15%+ return on capital within two years. Extends Volex's Medical business from equipment-internal cables to patient-connected products with minimal customer overlap.
retail_take: Strategically logical bolt-on acquisition of known asset with strong margins and cross-sell potential, immediately accretive and within capital allocation targets; likely market positive for execution on stated $2bn revenue growth plan.

headline: Turkiye Garanti Bankasi A.S.: Announcement regarding Corporate Governance Rating Agreement
Holding(s) in Company
brief: Nokia Bell Pensioenfonds OFP reduces stake in M.P. Evans Group from 9.99% to 8.92%.
summary: Belgian pension fund Nokia Bell Pensioenfonds OFP crossed a regulatory threshold on 14 July 2026 by reducing its voting rights holding in M.P. Evans Group PLC from 9.99% to 8.92%, representing 4,654,945 shares. The reduction was notified to the issuer on the same date. No financial instruments or indirect holdings are involved in the position.
retail_take: Routine major holding notification showing a modest stake reduction below the 9% threshold; typical institutional portfolio management activity with no obvious strategic implications.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Jeremy Attard-Manche acquires 6.76% stake in Ondo InsurTech PLC, crossing disclosure threshold.
summary: Jeremy Attard-Manche, based in Gudja, Malta, notified on 13 July 2026 of crossing a major shareholding threshold in Ondo InsurTech PLC on 10 July 2026. He now holds 16.67 million voting rights representing 6.76% of the company's issued share capital, acquired through direct share ownership with no financial instruments involved. This represents a material change in the company's ownership structure requiring regulatory notification under TR-1 disclosure rules.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major shareholding acquisition; market impact depends on Attard-Manche's identity and intentions, which are not detailed in this standard regulatory form.

headline: Sub-division
brief: Imaging Biometrics completes sub-division resulting in 246.7m ordinary shares of £0.005 each.
summary: Imaging Biometrics Limited has completed its sub-division, approved by the Jersey Financial Services Commission via Certificate of Registration. The restructuring resulted in 246,709,789 ordinary shares of £0.005 par value each. This follows earlier announcements on 12 May and 3 June regarding the proposed sub-division.
retail_take: Routine capital restructuring completion; sub-divisions typically neutral for existing shareholders as share counts increase while proportional ownership remains unchanged.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Bytes Technology Group directors Sam Mudd (CEO) and Gavin Rochussen (Independent Non-Executive Director) purchased shares on 13 July 2026.
summary: CEO Sam Mudd purchased 25,000 ordinary shares at 413.09p per share, while Independent Non-Executive Director Gavin Rochussen purchased 100,000 shares at 415.79p per share, both via on-market trades on the London Stock Exchange on 13 July 2026. These transactions were disclosed in accordance with UK Market Abuse Regulation Article 19 requirements.
retail_take: Director share purchases signal confidence in company prospects and can be viewed positively, though modest volumes and routine disclosure compliance mean limited market-moving impact.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Societe Generale crosses 6.2% voting rights threshold in Intertek Group PLC via share acquisition and CFDs.
summary: Societe Generale's voting rights in Intertek Group PLC increased to 6.203866% as of 10 July 2026, comprising 5.610022% from direct share ownership (8,635,608 shares) and 0.593844% from cash-settled contracts for difference expiring 20 June 2028. This represents an increase from the previous notification level of 5.371310%, triggering the mandatory disclosure requirement. The notification was filed on 13 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of threshold crossing by major financial institution; indicates passive investment position rather than strategic stake. Likely viewed as non-event absent activist intent signals.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: River Global PLC crosses 4.72% voting rights threshold in Liontrust Asset Management.
summary: River Global PLC acquired voting rights in Liontrust Asset Management PLC, crossing the 4.72% notification threshold on 30 June 2026. The holding comprises 2,970,232 ordinary shares with voting rights, held directly with no financial instruments involved. This represents River Global's first notifiable position in Liontrust, with the notification formally submitted to the issuer on 13 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a substantial shareholding. Without context on River Global's intentions or strategic rationale, the market will likely treat this as a non-event unless followed by further announcements.

headline: Result of AGM
brief: CelLBxHealth AGM approves all resolutions except director reappointment; P M Collins departed July 6.
summary: CelLBxHealth plc held its AGM on 14 July 2026, passing all resolutions except Resolution 4, which sought to reappoint director P M Collins. Collins left the business on 6 July 2026, rendering his reappointment moot. Voting details are available on the company's investor relations website. The AIM-listed circulating tumour cell (CTC) intelligence company specialises in the Parsortix platform for cancer research and clinical oncology applications.
retail_take: Routine governance matter with director departure. No material operational impact signalled; standard AGM outcome with one anticipated withdrawal due to prior resignation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Artemis UK Future Leaders repurchased 11,830 shares at 366p per share on 13 July 2026.
summary: Artemis UK Future Leaders Plc purchased 11,830 ordinary shares (0.02% of issued capital) at 366 pence per share on 13 July 2026, to be held in treasury. Following the purchase, issued share capital stands at 49,826,436 shares with 20,682,955 in treasury, leaving 29,143,481 shares with voting rights. The transaction updates the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations.
retail_take: Routine share buyback; modest scale and treasury holding suggest capital management rather than market timing signal. Minimal direct investor impact; non-event for price direction.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: BH Macro repurchases 13,732 Sterling shares at 4.24p on 13 July; shares held in treasury.
summary: BH Macro Limited purchased 13,732 of its own Sterling shares on 13 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities at a weighted average price of 4.2350p. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the transaction, total Sterling shares in issue (excluding treasury) number 300,194,258, with 77,561,454 held in treasury. Total voting rights in the company are now 459,519,951.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury holding; modest scale reflects standard capital management. No material read-through to investment strategy or performance.

company: Brown Advisory US Smaller Cos. PLC
headline: Portfolio Update
brief: Brown Advisory US Smaller Cos PLC releases month-end portfolio data as of 30 June 2026.
summary: The investment trust's top ten holdings represent 27.45% of total assets, led by Oceaneering International (3.79%), Establishment Labs (3.23%), and HealthEquity (3.10%). The portfolio is heavily weighted towards Industrials (32.46%) and Healthcare (21.97%), with Information Technology at 17.28%. Cash position stands at 5.46% of total assets. The trust maintains a diversified US smaller-cap strategy across 11 sectors.
retail_take: Routine portfolio disclosure with no material changes signalled; investors can assess sector positioning and holding concentration, but no strategic shift or performance commentary provided.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GCP Infrastructure repurchased 3.57m shares at 83.55p average on 13 July under existing buyback authority.
summary: GCP Infrastructure Investments Ltd purchased 3,570,000 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of 83.55p through Canaccord Genuity Limited. The shares were bought within a range of 82.91p to 83.70p. This transaction is part of an ongoing buyback programme announced in December 2024, under which the company has cumulatively repurchased 79,623,657 shares for treasury. Following the purchase, 788,188,993 voting rights remain in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under existing shareholder authority; consistent with capital management strategy signalling confidence but offering no new strategic insight to investors.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Jim Mellon acquires 33.5m Agronomics shares (18.3% stake) for £1.49m plus market purchases.
summary: Agronomics' Executive Chair Jim Mellon purchased 27.49m ordinary shares from portfolio company BlueNalu for US$2m (£1.49m) at 5.43p per share on 13 July 2026, plus 6.05m shares via brokers between March 2026 at 6.6-7.0p. Combined with existing holdings, Mellon now controls 199.3m shares (18.30% voting rights). The BlueNalu transaction avoids public market sales and provides working capital. Mellon receives right of first refusal on remaining 3.15m BlueNalu shares (expires 1 October 2026) and becomes BlueNalu board director. Shares currently trade at 56% discount to 12.93p net asset value.
retail_take: Insider buying at significant discount to NAV suggests confidence, but large concentrated stake and orderly market restrictions may constrain near-term upside. Mixed read: positive signal offset by structural constraints.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Harwood Capital entities increase Capita stake to 5.18% of voting rights, crossing disclosure threshold.
summary: Harwood Capital LLP and its associated entities (Rockwood Strategic Plc and Oryx International Growth Fund Limited), ultimately controlled by Christopher Harwood and Bernard Mills, notified on 13 July 2026 of a shareholding increase to 5.181% (6.26m voting rights) in Capita PLC as of 10 July 2026, up from 4.56%. The holding comprises 10,000 direct shares via Harwood Capital LLP, 5.5m indirect votes via Rockwood Strategic (4.552%), and 750,000 via Oryx (0.621%).
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of a major holding crossing 5% threshold; no indication of activist intent or strategic change; market likely to treat as administrative notification with minimal impact.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Lord John Lee of Trafford's stake in Christie Group falls to 5.82% from 6.30% following fund beneficiary death.
summary: Lord Lee of Trafford's holding in Christie Group PLC (AIM: CTG.L) decreased to 1,542,915 ordinary shares (5.82% of issued capital) from the previously notified 6.30%, effective 9 July 2026. The reduction resulted from his cessation of discretionary investment management over a fund following the beneficiary's death, with voting rights and investment decisions transferring to the estate's executors. The notification was disclosed 10 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure reflecting a technical change in control structure rather than a market-driven transaction; no material implications for shareholders or company direction.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
brief: India Capital Growth Fund repurchased 25,000 shares at 171p per share on 13 July 2026.
summary: India Capital Growth Fund Limited purchased 25,000 ordinary shares for treasury at 171p each on 13 July 2026. Following the transaction, voting shares in issue total 65,372,624 (excluding 47.1m treasury shares). The fund manages £118.6m in assets, invests in Indian mid and small-cap listed companies, and is managed by River Global Investors (now part of Liontrust). The fund initiated interim dividend payments commencing October 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; typical capital management action for closed-end funds. No material market implications unless part of broader capital strategy.

headline: Engagement with dissenting shareholders
brief: Old Mutual to engage with shareholders over failed remuneration votes that fell short of 75% threshold.
summary: Old Mutual's Remuneration Policy and Remuneration Implementation Report received majority shareholder support at the 5 June 2026 AGM but fell short of the 75% advisory voting threshold required under JSE Listings Requirements, achieving 68.39% and 70.72% respectively. The company is now engaging dissenting shareholders via virtual call on 29 July 2026 at 15:30 South African time, with shareholders invited to submit concerns in writing by 24 July to investorrelations@oldmutual.com.
retail_take: Remuneration votes failing to meet advisory thresholds signals shareholder dissatisfaction with executive pay; engagement exercise is standard governance practice but indicates investor concerns requiring management attention.

headline: Exploration permit application in New Zealand
brief: Sunda Energy applies for 645 km² offshore NZ exploration permit covering Awakino gas field as part of wider growth strategy following Matahio NZ conditional acquisition.
summary: Sunda Energy submitted a Petroleum Exploration Permit application for a 645 km² offshore Taranaki Basin area containing the Awakino gas condensate field (originally drilled 1985). The application has been accepted by New Zealand Petroleum and Minerals and enters a 3-month competitive period. Firm commitments include technical studies and reprocessing 450 km² of 3D seismic data within 36 months. This supports Sunda's April 2026 conditional acquisition of Matahio Energy NZ Limited and broader New Zealand gas exploration, development and production strategy.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration permit application with no certainty of award given pending competitive process; aligns with stated NZ strategy but represents optionality rather than near-term value catalyst; market likely neutral to marginally positive on strategic progress.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Tekcapital Europe Limited's voting stake in MicroSalt PLC decreased from 58.26% to 57.84% following share disposal on 6 July 2026.
summary: Tekcapital Europe Limited (controlled by Tekcapital plc) notified the FCA on 10 July 2026 of a change in its major holding in MicroSalt PLC. The stake fell from 58.26% to 57.84%, representing 32,457,535 voting rights, following an acquisition or disposal of voting rights on 6 July 2026. The controlling entity Tekcapital plc maintains a 100% interest in Tekcapital Europe Limited.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of major shareholding movement; modest stake reduction suggests controlled shareholder activity rather than strategic shift. Likely non-event for equity markets given minimal percentage change.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Tekcapital Europe Limited's stake in MicroSalt PLC fell to 57.84% from 58.26% following a disposal of voting rights on 6 July 2026.
summary: Tekcapital Europe Limited (company number 08121738) notified the FCA on 10 July 2026 of a change in its holding in MicroSalt PLC. The company's voting rights decreased to 57.84% (32,457,535 shares) from 58.26%, triggered by an acquisition or disposal of voting rights on 6 July 2026. Tekcapital plc is the ultimate controlling entity holding 100% of Tekcapital Europe Limited. No financial instruments are involved.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a modest stake reduction by the major shareholder; no material implications for retail investors given the controlling position remains unchanged.

headline: Director Shareholding & Issue of Equity
brief: Cobra Resources chairman Andrew Michelmore invests £140k; company issues options, warrants and acquires Manna Hill copper project.
summary: Non-Executive Chairman Andrew Michelmore subscribed for 3.5m ordinary shares at £0.04 per share (£140k investment), signalling confidence in the South Australian rare earth and copper explorer. Directors and employees received 16.5m options at 6p exercise price; 4m warrants issued to project stakeholders at 8p. Cobra exercised its Manna Hill Option, issuing 12.1m consideration shares at 4.34p to acquire 100% of the Manna Hill copper project, expanding its portfolio alongside the Boland rare earth discovery. Admission of new shares expected 17 July 2026.
retail_take: Chairman's material personal investment and aggressive project expansion via equity issuance signals management conviction but increases shareholder dilution; positive signal offset by dilution concerns typical of junior explorers.

headline: Appointment of Non-Executive Director - Update
brief: Clive Norman appointed as Non-Executive Director to BRCK Group PLC board with immediate effect following due diligence completion.
summary: Clive Stanley Norman, aged 76, has joined BRCK Group plc's Board as Non-Executive Director effective immediately, following completion of due diligence processes initiated by the 19 June 2026 announcement. Norman currently holds 2,007,096 ordinary shares (0.62% of issued capital) and has previous directorship experience across multiple construction and specialist product companies. He maintains current directorships at Beyond Number Five Limited and Clive S Norman and Associates Limited.
retail_take: Routine board appointment with material shareholder alignment; Norman's existing 0.62% stake and construction industry experience suggest confidence in strategy, though non-executive role limits immediate operational impact.

headline: Notice of Results
brief: Gateley to announce FY2026 results on 21 July 2026, confirming scheduled disclosure timeline.
summary: Gateley (Holdings) PLC, the AIM-listed professional services group, will release its full year results for the period ended 30 April 2026 on Tuesday 21 July 2026. This is a notice of results announcement confirming the date and contact details for investor enquiries and media relations.
retail_take: Routine scheduling announcement. Market will await actual results on 21 July for substantive trading updates, margins and forward guidance rather than reading into this notice itself.

headline: Notice of Annual General Meeting
brief: Marks Electrical Group plc announces 2026 AGM scheduled for 6 August at Leicester registered office.
summary: Marks Electrical Group plc's Annual General Meeting will be held on 6 August 2026 at 12:30 p.m. at 4 Boston Road, Leicester, LE4 1AU. The Board encourages shareholders to vote by proxy before the 4 August 2026 deadline at 12:30 p.m. Shareholders planning to attend should notify the Company Secretary by close of business on 4 August 2026. Notice of AGM documents are available on the corporate website.
retail_take: Routine procedural announcement for shareholder meeting; no material business developments disclosed or implied.

headline: Equity Subscription, TVR and PDMR Dealings
brief: Insig AI raises £350k from CEO and existing shareholder; CEO's stake rises to 18.6% post-subscription.
summary: Insig AI accepted £250k equity subscription from CEO Richard Bernstein at 15p per share (1.67m shares), representing 9.1% premium to 13.75p closing price on 13 July. Bernstein's holding increases to 24.1m shares (18.6% post-subscription). Company also raised £100k from existing high-net-worth shareholder at same 15p price. Board granted Bernstein a call option for additional £250k at 18p within six months. Funds allocated to working capital and digital asset investments. 2.33m shares admitted to AIM trading around 20 July; enlarged voting rights total 129.9m shares.
retail_take: CEO backing with fresh capital signals confidence in digital asset strategy and attracts external investor interest; related-party transaction flagged as fair by independent directors, suggesting governance oversight maintained.

headline: First Helium Sold from Pinon Canyon Plant
brief: Helium One's Colorado joint venture sells first helium production from Pinon Canyon Plant, reaching commercial milestone.
summary: Blue Star Helium (operator) sold the first production tube trailer from the Pinon Canyon Plant following commissioning completion. A second trailer under a 3-month fixed-price offtake agreement announced 4 June 2026 is now on-site. Plant uptime and fill rates have improved significantly. The operator plans to drill three new wells in H2 2026 and deepen existing wells to increase throughput. Strong North American helium demand persists, driven by semiconductor, aerospace and defence sectors, with supply constraints supporting US domestic production.
retail_take: First commercial helium revenue validates the Colorado project's viability and de-risks Helium One's diversification strategy, though 50% working interest means proportional benefit; market likely reads as positive progression toward multi-asset production.

headline: Customer Agreement Update
brief: Itaconix secures first customer agreement for plastic-free dish detergent tablets in North America.
summary: Itaconix announced its first customer supply agreement for 8-gram plastic-free dish detergent tablets under its SPARX Formulated Solutions business. The tablets, developed using Itaconix TSI polymers and Bonals tablet press technology, will be supplied to a North American purpose-driven brand from the company's Innovation Center pilot-scale capacity. The product was showcased at the American Cleaning Institute's Innovation Showcase in February 2026 and is available via deeperclean.com, with retail availability expected by end-2026.
retail_take: Commercial validation of Itaconix's plastic-free tablet technology with initial North American customer represents meaningful market traction, though volumes and financial impact remain unspecified, making this a positive but modest milestone.

headline: AstraZeneca licenses novel EGFR inhibitor
brief: AstraZeneca acquires global rights to Zegfrovy, a novel oral EGFR inhibitor for lung cancer, via $600m upfront deal with Dizal Pharmaceutical.
summary: AstraZeneca licensed Zegfrovy (sunvozertinib) from Dizal Pharmaceutical for $600m upfront plus up to $900m in milestone payments and tiered royalties. The oral irreversible EGFR inhibitor treats NSCLC with exon 20 insertion mutations—affecting ~25% of EGFR-mutated lung cancer patients globally. Already approved in US and China for post-platinum-based chemotherapy, Zegfrovy showed positive Phase III data in first-line treatment presented at ASCO 2026, with FDA and China regulatory breakthrough designations granted. Transaction closes H2 2026; no impact on 2026 guidance.
retail_take: Positive: Strengthens AstraZeneca's leading EGFR-mutation lung cancer portfolio with a differentiated oral therapy addressing an underserved patient population with limited options and regulatory momentum.

headline: Notice of Interim Results
brief: Irish Residential Properties REIT schedules H1 2026 interim results release and investor call for 14 August.
summary: I-RES will release its interim results for the six months to 30 June 2026 at 7:00am GMT on Friday 14 August 2026, followed by a management conference call at 9:00am GMT. The investor call will include live webcast access and presentation slides, with dial-in numbers provided for Ireland, UK, and global locations. A replay facility will be available until 28 August 2026. This is a standard notification of financial reporting timing for the Irish residential property REIT.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of results timing; no material information released. Market will await actual H1 figures and management commentary on Irish residential property market conditions on 14 August.

headline: Q2 2026 Operations Update
brief: Atalaya Mining delivers strong Q2 production with improved grades and significantly increased cash position to €351.2m.
summary: Copper production reached 13,493 tonnes in Q2 2026 versus 9,939 tonnes in Q1, with copper grade improving to 0.39% and recovery at 83.91%. Net cash position surged to €340.2m from €122.0m year-end 2025, supported by realised copper prices averaging US$6.14/lb. Company maintains FY2026 guidance of 50,000-54,000 tonnes production at low end; modest cost impacts from Middle East conflicts with potential US$0.15-0.20/lb upside risk. Proyecto Touro permitting nearing completion; Swedish exploration projects showing strong VMS intercepts.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum and substantially improved balance sheet in tight copper market likely appeal to growth-focused investors; guidance prudence and geopolitical cost hedging suggest disciplined management, though production guidance at low end warrants attention.

headline: Appointment of Director
brief: Angus Energy appoints Ross Pearson as Chief Operating Officer and Executive Director with immediate effect.
summary: Ross Pearson, aged 49, has been promoted from Director of Wells and Subsurface (a role he held since October 2023) to Chief Operating Officer and Executive Director of Angus Energy PLC. He brings 25 years of E&P experience, including previous tenure as Technical Director at IGas Energy plc. In his new role, Pearson assumes board-level responsibility for operational, technical and HSE functions. He currently holds 12,500,000 ordinary shares (0.16% of issued capital) following a subscription announced on 24 June 2026.
retail_take: Internal promotion of experienced technical executive to operational leadership is mildly positive, signalling management stability and continuity, though routine for a director appointment with no material transaction or strategic shift involved.

headline: Notification of Shareholdings
brief: Titon Holdings notifies of shareholding changes following estate settlement; two individuals now hold ~8.19% combined.
summary: Following settlement of the late John Anderson's estate on 6 July 2026, his entire holding of 868,902 ordinary shares in Titon Holdings PLC was transferred to Tyson Anderson and Kimberly Anderson. Tyson Anderson holds 434,451 shares (3.86% of issued capital) and Kimberly Anderson holds 487,451 shares (4.33% of issued capital), with notifications made on 11 and 13 July respectively.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of beneficial ownership change due to estate administration; no strategic implications apparent and unlikely to move markets materially.

headline: Appointment of Non-Executive Chair
brief: Celsius Resources appoints veteran mining executive George Bujtor as Non-Executive Chair, effective immediately.
summary: George Bujtor, a 76-year-old mining executive with 50+ years' experience including 25 years at Rio Tinto and senior roles in Philippine mining operations, has been appointed Non-Executive Chair on A$100,000 per annum base fee. He brings expertise from successfully funding and developing projects including the Berong Nickel Laterite Project and Carmen Copper Project. Peter Hume steps down as Interim Chair but remains on the Board. Bujtor stated focus on resolving MMCI arbitration and advancing the MCB Project toward development.
retail_take: Appointment of experienced Philippine mining sector specialist signals management confidence in MCB Project development and suggests potential progress on arbitration; positive signal for near-term catalysts and project advancement.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: The Lang Family and associated entities crossed a 3.04% voting rights threshold in Coro Energy PLC on 10 July 2026.
summary: The Lang Family, through G.P. (Jersey) Limited and Frazer Lang, notified the FCA on 12 July 2026 that they acquired voting rights in Coro Energy PLC, crossing the 3% disclosure threshold. The resulting position totals 3,300,000 voting rights representing 3.04% of the company's voting rights, acquired through direct share ownership. The threshold was crossed on 10 July 2026.
retail_take: Routine major holding disclosure showing a significant shareholder position just above the 3% notification threshold; no control implications suggested, likely a non-event for equity trading.

headline: Director Dealing
brief: SysGroup Executive Chairman Heejae Chae purchases 416,639 shares at 24p, increasing stake to 13.17%.
summary: Executive Chairman Heejae Chae bought 416,639 ordinary shares at 24 pence per share on 13 July 2026, raising his beneficial holding to 11,025,709 shares (13.17% of voting rights). The purchase occurred on the London Stock Exchange. SysGroup is a UK Managed IT Service Provider specialising in cybersecurity, managed services, and AI-enabled solutions for mid-market clients, with offices across Edinburgh, London, Manchester, and Newport.
retail_take: Director share purchase signals confidence in company prospects, though routine disclosure with limited immediate market impact absent broader context on valuation or strategic developments.

headline: Director/PDMR Shareholding
brief: Physiomics director Ian Bagnall acquires 1.1m shares at 0.45p, raising stake to 3.55%.
summary: Ian Bagnall, Executive Director of Physiomics plc (AIM: PYC), purchased 1,111,111 ordinary shares at 0.45p per share on 10 July 2026 via market purchase. His total holding now stands at 16,111,111 shares, representing approximately 3.55% of issued share capital. Physiomics provides mathematical modelling, biostatistics and data science services to pharmaceutical companies, having modelled over 125 targets and drugs across 140+ projects.
retail_take: Director's substantial open-market purchase signals confidence in the company's valuation and prospects, though the transaction scale is modest relative to market cap. Likely viewed positively by markets as a vote of confidence.

headline: Chair Succession
brief: Sir Ian Cheshire steps down as Chair by 30 September 2026; Debbie White, current Senior Independent Director, appointed Chair-Designate.
summary: Sir Ian Cheshire will exit the Chair role by 30 September 2026 after five years, citing new commitments as Chair of Ofcom and newly appointed Chair of Anglian Water. Debbie White, who joined the Board as independent non-executive director in February 2023 and became senior independent director in May 2023, will become Chair-Designate immediately and Chair from Cheshire's departure date. Natalie Ceeney will assume the Senior Independent Director role. The Board conducted a thorough selection process and was unanimous in choosing White, citing her sector expertise and stakeholder understanding.
retail_take: Orderly succession with internal promotion of experienced board member signals continuity and stability; market likely to view positively as non-disruptive leadership transition with clear process and timeline.

headline: Board Appointment
brief: Genedrive appoints Graham Cole CBE as Chair, a highly experienced aerospace and manufacturing executive with 40+ years leadership.
summary: Graham Cole CBE, aged 77, has joined Genedrive PLC as Chair and independent Non-Executive Director effective immediately. Cole brings four decades of UK and international leadership experience, previously chairing Westland Helicopters (£4bn revenues) and holding senior roles within AgustaWestland Group. He was appointed CBE in 2004 for aerospace services and holds Freedom to the City of London. The company is also recruiting two further independent Non-Executive Directors. Cole holds no ordinary shares in Genedrive.
retail_take: Strong appointment signals confidence in growth strategy and governance strengthening. Cole's heavyweight aerospace/manufacturing background and government connections could enhance credibility and unlock strategic opportunities, though age (77) and lack of shareholding warrant noting.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: Premier Miton Group crosses 5.37% voting rights threshold in Potentially AI PLC following reverse takeover.
summary: Premier Miton Group plc has acquired 20,560,114 voting rights representing 5.372543% of Potentially AI PLC (formerly Tiger Alpha Plc), crossing the notifiable threshold on 13 July 2026. The holding was notified on the same date and results from a reverse takeover transaction under AIM Rule 14. The voting rights are held directly through a chain of controlled undertakings including Premier Asset Management entities.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a major holding notification following a structured corporate transaction; expected market event with no apparent negative implications.

headline: Notice of Full Year Results
brief: Naked Wines to release FY26 audited results on 23 July 2026 with investor presentation and Q&A.
summary: Naked Wines plc will publish audited full year results for the 52 weeks ended 30 March 2026 on Thursday, 23 July 2026. An investor presentation will be held at 14:00 BST via the Investor Meet Company platform, followed by a live Q&A session open to all investors. Pre-event questions can be submitted until 09:00 BST on 23 July, with a recording subsequently available on the company's investor website.
retail_take: Routine announcement of results publication date and investor presentation logistics; no material information disclosed; market read will depend entirely on the results themselves when released.

headline: Director Declaration
brief: Sarah Warby, Mony Group non-executive director, to join Taylor Wimpey plc board from 2 October 2026.
summary: Sarah Warby, a non-executive director at Mony Group PLC, will take on an additional non-executive director role at Taylor Wimpey plc effective 2 October 2026. The announcement is made in compliance with UKLR 6.4.9(2) regulatory requirements. This represents a standard disclosure of a director's external appointment.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of director appointment elsewhere; no material implications for Mony Group shareholders or operations evident.

headline: Production Report Q3 2026 ended 30 June 2026
brief: Tharisa reports Q3 FY2026 production on track with PGM output up 15.5% and net cash of $10.7m despite increased capex.
summary: PGM production reached 39.6 koz in Q3 (up 15.5% QoQ) with improved recovery at 83.8%, while chrome production was 393.8 kt. PGM basket prices averaged $2,681/oz (down 11.8% QoQ but up 70% year-on-year), and chrome prices strengthened at $306/t. Cash on hand increased to $198.8m but debt rose to $188.1m due to planned capex for Karo Platinum and underground transition projects. Net cash position declined to $10.7m from $54.7m. Tharisa remains on track to deliver FY2026 guidance of 145-165 koz PGM and 1.50-1.65 Mt chrome production.
retail_take: Solid operational delivery with production gains and maintained development momentum offset by rising capex-driven debt, but net cash remains positive; likely read as constructive given guidance reaffirmation and two growth projects advancing on schedule.

headline: Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
brief: FRP Advisory Group hosting full year results presentation via Investor Meet Company on 24 July at 10:00 BST.
summary: FRP Advisory Group PLC will present its full year results to existing and potential shareholders via Investor Meet Company on 24 July 2026 at 10:00 BST. The presentation is open to all investors who can register for free on the platform. Pre-event questions can be submitted until 23 July 09:00 BST, with further questions accepted during the live presentation.
retail_take: Routine announcement of investor engagement activity ahead of results; no material disclosure but suggests management engagement with shareholder base.

headline: Post-Close Statement and Board Update
brief: JPEL updates board succession, cuts annual costs to $150k, and explores strategic options including potential liquidation.
summary: JPEL has completed its board refreshment with Roy Barrett joining as Chair Designate in April 2026, set to assume the role in October when Tony Dalwood steps down. Annual administrative expenses have been reduced from approximately $400,000 (2025) to $150,000 effective January 2026. The board is assessing strategic options including formal liquidation timing, self-management, or continuing current operations as the vehicle size reduces. The company has returned over $500 million to shareholders since 2016 through portfolio realisations.
retail_take: Positive operational signals—cost discipline and board renewal—but uncertainty around strategic direction and potential liquidation could create near-term listing volatility for retail holders.

headline: Appointment of Transaction Advisers
brief: ZCCM-IH appoints Bowsprit Partners and Stockbrokers Zambia as transaction advisers for listing optimisation process.
summary: ZCCM Investments Holdings PLC appointed Bowsprit Partners Limited as UK Financial Adviser & Corporate Broker and Stockbrokers Zambia Limited as LuSE Advisor and Sponsoring Broker for its listing optimisation process. CEO Kakenenwa Muyangwa outlined long-term growth strategy targeting market capitalisation increase from US$1.4 billion to US$7 billion over the next decade, with plans to increase copper production, broaden international shareholder base, and enhance access to international capital markets.
retail_take: Positive signal: appointment of experienced advisers and articulated 5x valuation ambition suggest serious strategic intent to improve listing profile and capital access, supporting growth trajectory.

headline: Placing to raise £3.5 million
brief: Poolbeg Pharma raises £3.5m via placing of 58.3m shares at 6p, extending cash runway to Q2 2028.
summary: Poolbeg Pharma (AIM: POLB) raised £3.5m before expenses through a placing of 58,333,328 ordinary shares at 6p per share—a 16.08% discount to the prior day's closing price of 7.15p. The placing shares represent 8.27% dilution. Proceeds fund the POLB 001 TOPICAL cancer immunotherapy trial and oral GLP-1 obesity treatment proof-of-concept study through completion, with combined existing cash extending runway to Q2 2028. Executive Chair Cathal Friel subscribed £250,000. Admission expected 21 July 2026. Johnson & Johnson is supplying teclistamab free to the TOPICAL trial.
retail_take: Positive signal: capital raise at modest discount validates investor confidence in lead asset POLB 001; interim trial data due late summer could be key catalyst for pharma partnerships and further value creation.

headline: Results of Meeting
brief: GreenX Metals issues 2.1m unlisted options to directors following shareholder approval at general meeting.
summary: GreenX Metals held a general meeting on 14 July 2026 where shareholders approved two resolutions to issue incentive options. Benjamin Stoikovich received 1.5m options exercisable at A$1.50 by 31 May 2031 (99% vote in favour), while Mark Pearce received 600,000 options at the same strike price (99% approval, with 2.9m shares abstaining). Post-issuance, GreenX has 311.3m ordinary shares and 29m total options outstanding across various exercise prices and expiry dates.
retail_take: Routine shareholder approval for director remuneration via option grants; high voting support indicates investor confidence in management retention strategy, though no material operational substance implied.

headline: Coinsilium Group Limited: Predictive Labs Unveils Nijinn; Coinsilium Increases Strategic Investment and Accelerates Venture-Building Strategy
Delay in FY26 Audited Financial Statement
Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
brief: Northern Bear to announce FY2026 results on 16 July with investor presentation scheduled for 21 July.
summary: Northern Bear Plc will release audited results for the year ended 31 March 2026 on 16 July 2026 at 07:00 BST. CEO John Davies and Interim FD Jim Waring will host a live presentation on 21 July 2026 at 13:00 BST via Investor Meet Company, open to all shareholders. Investors can pre-submit questions until 20 July 09:00 BST or participate live.
retail_take: Routine results announcement with standard investor engagement; no material substance disclosed to indicate positive or negative direction ahead of results.

headline: Notice of Interim Results & Investor Presentation
brief: Audioboom to publish H1 2026 interim results on 15 July with live investor presentation at 3pm via Investor Meet Company.
summary: Audioboom Group PLC will release interim results for the six months ending 30 June 2026 on 15 July 2026. CEO Stuart Last and CFO Brad Clarke will host a live presentation at 3pm via Investor Meet Company platform, open to existing and potential shareholders. Investors can submit questions pre-event or during the presentation. The global podcast company reports 180 million monthly downloads across 58 million unique listeners and partners with premium content including Formula 1 official podcasts.
retail_take: Routine results announcement with standard investor engagement; no material disclosures, substance of performance to follow on publication date.

headline: RM plc: Interim Results
brief: RM plc reports H1 results with adjusted operating profit up 200% to £2.7m, though overall revenue down 4.2% to £70.1m.
summary: EdTech firm RM plc posted adjusted operating profit of £2.7m (HY25: £0.9m) and adjusted EBITDA of £5.2m (HY25: £3.5m) for the six months ended 31 May 2026. Revenue fell 4.2% to £70.1m due to UK schools' market challenges in the Technology division. Assessment division showed strength with recurring revenue up 7.3% to £19.0m and operating margin at 25.9%, with 100% customer renewal rate. Net debt remained flat at £59.3m with £3.7m invested in RM Ava platform during the period. FY26 adjusted operating profit expected to meet market expectations of £13.6m.
retail_take: Positive read: Assessment turnaround and margin expansion offset legacy divisional weakness; strategic transformation on track with separation complete and Ava investment justified by 100%+ pipeline growth. Debt flat despite investment signals disciplined capital management.

headline: GPE sets standard for materials reuse
brief: GPE demonstrates circular economy leadership at 30 Duke Street, reusing 450 tonnes of steel and other materials at scale.
summary: Great Portland Estates showcased industry-leading circular building practices at its 30 Duke Street development, part of the King-backed 'Circularity in Practice' initiative. The project reused 450 tonnes of structural steel from another GPE site, returned 400+ tonnes to market, incorporated 45 tonnes of recycled aluminium, and repurposed 21 tonnes of Portland stone and original marble. Across three developments, GPE has reused 500+ tonnes of steel and diverted 91 tonnes of glass from waste. The building is fully pre-let to The Crown Estate, demonstrating commercial viability of circular practices.
retail_take: Positive sentiment likely as demonstration of ESG credentials ahead of practical completion and strong lettings, aligning with investor focus on sustainability and operational execution.

headline: Notice of F27 First Quarter Results Date
brief: Wizz Air to release Q1 F27 results on 6 August 2026 with analyst webcast at 09:30 BST.
summary: Wizz Air Holdings will publish unaudited first quarter results for the three months ended 30 June 2026 on Thursday, 6 August 2026 at 07:00 BST. Management will host a live webcast presentation for analysts and institutional investors at 09:30 BST the same day, with a recording subsequently available on the company's website. The airline operates 264 Airbus aircraft and served 69.7 million passengers in the 2026 financial year.
retail_take: Routine earnings announcement; standard quarterly results disclosure with typical investor presentation mechanics. Market will await actual financial results and guidance rather than read-through from this notice.

headline: Notification of Q1 Statement
brief: Big Yellow Group to release Q1 2026 results on 20 July covering period to 30 June.
summary: Big Yellow Group PLC, the UK's self-storage market leader, will publish its first quarter statement for the period ended 30 June 2026 on Monday, 20 July 2026. The announcement provides advance notification of the results release date. No financial figures or trading updates are disclosed in this notification.
retail_take: Routine results calendar announcement; investors should await the actual Q1 statement on 20 July for material trading performance and financial metrics.

headline: Posting of Circular
brief: Technology Minerals posts circular ahead of AGM on 17 July covering share subdivision and proposed capital reorganisation.
summary: Technology Minerals is posting a circular to shareholders providing details on steps following its AGM on 17 July 2026. The company proposes subdividing its 3.14 billion ordinary shares (nominal value £0.001) into 6.29 billion shares (£0.0005 each) to enable a fundraise and settlement of convertible loan notes. Post-subdivision, the board intends to create a new Deferred Share class with no voting or dividend rights, with existing shareholders receiving one Deferred Share each alongside their ordinary holdings. The FCA prospectus for the proposed placing is anticipated shortly.
retail_take: Material corporate action enabling capital raise and debt settlement; share subdivision dilutes existing shareholders but Deferred Share structure preserves voting equality. Market likely neutral-to-positive if fundraise strengthens balance sheet, though dilution warrants scrutiny.

headline: Results of AGM
brief: Ampeak Energy AGM approves all resolutions; appoints Mark Dawber as Non-Executive Director and David Taaffe as Executive Director.
summary: All 13 resolutions passed at Ampeak Energy's AGM on 14 July 2026 with strong shareholder support. Mark Dawber (38) joined as Non-Executive Director; David Taaffe (45), Chief Operating Officer, joined as Executive Director. Taaffe holds 20.2m options over ordinary shares. One nominee, Scott Martin, resigned immediately after approval as SUEH nominated only a single appointee. Auditor Nexia Singapore PAC reappointed with 99.90% approval. Directors' remuneration reports passed with 88.87% support. Share issuance authority approved at 95.01%.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure with routine board appointments and strong shareholder support across all resolutions. Likely viewed as administrative formality with no material implications for share price or strategy execution.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: Ashmore AuM rises 7% to $54bn in Q4 FY2026 on strong EM performance and net inflows.
summary: Ashmore Group's assets under management increased by US$3.3 billion to US$54.0 billion in the quarter ending 30 June 2026, driven by US$2.0 billion in positive investment performance and US$1.3 billion in net inflows. Fixed income grew to US$42.0 billion while equities and alternatives expanded. Net inflows were delivered across local currency, equities, blended and corporate debt themes. Emerging markets indices delivered strong returns (fixed income +2-5%, large cap equities +24%), and the firm maintained outperformance across strategies relative to benchmarks.
retail_take: Positive read: demonstrating client confidence in EM assets, solid organic growth, and sustained alpha generation despite geopolitical volatility around the Strait of Hormuz, supporting the investment case for Ashmore's active management differentiation.

headline: FY26 Trading Update – Ahead of expectations
brief: Genus PLC reports FY26 results moderately ahead of consensus at £98m adjusted PBT, with strong cash generation and significantly reduced leverage.
summary: Animal genetics company Genus delivered FY26 adjusted profit before tax of approximately £98m, ahead of consensus range of £94.3m-£96.9m. PIC segment performed strongly in Asia and Latin America despite North American headwinds, while ABS achieved double-digit profit growth. The company received £111m net cash from Chinese joint venture partner BCA in Q4, reducing year-end net leverage to approximately 0.4x from higher levels. Preliminary results announced 10 September 2026.
retail_take: Positive read: results beat expectations, cash generation was strong, and balance sheet significantly de-leveraged, positioning the company well for FY27 with reduced financial risk.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Experian repurchases 225,940 shares at average price of 2,628.95p under ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Experian plc purchased 225,940 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from 2,589p to 2,678p, with a weighted average price of 2,628.95p. The shares will be cancelled. This follows the company's 30 June 2026 announcement of a share repurchase programme, with weekly publication of transaction details on its website.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks are generally market-neutral signals of confidence and capital deployment, but this is standard programme execution with no material read-through.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 40,000 shares at average price of $165.17 on 13 July, to be cancelled.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 40,000 ordinary shares via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange on 13 July 2026 at prices ranging from $163.40 to $166.45, with a volume-weighted average price of $165.1709 per share. The shares will be cancelled. Following the transaction, IHG has 148,615,326 shares in issue, excluding 5.43 million held in treasury. The purchase was executed under the shareholder authority granted at the May 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Routine share buyback with subsequent cancellation to reduce share count and potentially boost EPS; typical capital management activity with no material news content or operational implications.

headline: 2Q26 bp Trading Statement Part 1 of 1
brief: BP Q2 2026 trading update shows production headwinds offset by strong commodity prices and refining margins.
summary: BP expects Q2 2026 upstream production of 2,170–2,220 mboe/d (down from 2,339 in Q1) due to Gulf of America seasonal maintenance and Middle East disruption. Oil realizations are forecast +$1.8–2.1bn and gas +$0.5–0.7bn versus Q1. Refining throughput expected 1,445–1,475 mb/d with margins +$1.2–1.4bn. Brent averaged $103.85/bbl in Q2 (vs $81.13 Q1); RIM at $29.6/bbl (vs $16.9). Net debt forecast $22–23bn, down from $25.3bn post-hybrid bond redemption. Tax rate 33–37%; exploration write-offs ~$0.5bn. Full results 4 August.
retail_take: Mixed signal: production declines but strong price environment and margin uplift offset weakness. Market likely neutral-to-slightly-positive on improved cash generation and deleveraging, though geopolitical and seasonal disruption risks remain.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Grafton Group purchases 60,000 shares for cancellation on 13 July as part of £25m buyback programme.
summary: Grafton Group PLC repurchased 60,000 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at a volume-weighted average price of £9.0106 per share, with a trading range of £8.99–£9.049. This transaction is part of the company's £25m share buyback programme announced 30 June 2026. To date, 599,174 shares have been purchased for cancellation through Goodbody Stockbrokers and Deutsche Bank since the programme commenced.
retail_take: Routine capital management; share buybacks typically signal confidence in valuation and support earnings per share, though market impact depends on overall financial health and growth prospects.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Sabre Insurance completes buyback, repurchasing 483,635 shares at average 181.48p for cancellation.
summary: Sabre Insurance Group PLC has purchased 483,635 ordinary shares for cancellation between 7-13 July 2026 under its shareholder-approved buyback programme announced 2 June 2026. Shares were bought at prices ranging from 179.40p to 183.00p, with an aggregate average price of 181.48p. Following cancellation, total issued shares reduce to 244,643,201. Panmure Liberum Limited executed trades across multiple venues (XLON, BATE, CHIX, TRQX).
retail_take: Routine capital management action reducing share count; typical EPS accretion play with no strategic implications. Market likely views neutrally as execution of pre-announced programme within tight price band suggests disciplined execution.

headline: Ofwat confirms £30.5m package for South East Water
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Greencoat Renewables repurchased 251,586 shares at €0.75-€0.76 as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: On 13 July 2026, Greencoat Renewables PLC purchased 251,586 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin via RBC Europe Limited at prices ranging from €0.7500 to €0.7630, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7576. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,083,937,464 shares in issue. This purchase forms part of the buyback programme announced on 5 March 2026.
retail_take: Share buybacks at modest valuations typically signal management confidence in intrinsic value and support earnings per share accretion; routine capital management execution.

headline: AGM Trading Update
brief: Sealand Capital Galaxy holds AGM; board encouraged by H1 progress and expects full-year performance in line with expectations.
summary: Sealand Capital Galaxy Limited (LSE: SCGL) held its AGM on 14 July 2026. The board reported encouragement from first-half progress and believes the group is well-positioned to execute its long-term strategy, supported by a strengthened balance sheet. The company remains focused on expanding technology capabilities across Asia-Pacific with emphasis on AI-enabled solutions and infrastructure, while exploring new commercial opportunities. The board expects current financial year performance to align with expectations and will provide further updates upon publishing half-year results to 30 June 2026.
retail_take: Cautiously optimistic trading update with no specific metrics, suggesting steady performance but offering limited visibility. Board confidence in strategy execution and balance sheet strength provides some reassurance despite macroeconomic headwinds acknowledged.

headline: Operational Update, Off Take & Mineralogical Study
brief: Ironveld achieves sustained DMS plant production with three secured clients and strong demand; Layer 21 study progressing on schedule.
summary: Ironveld's DMS plant has entered sustained production, operating 16 hours daily at 2.5-3.5 tph (design capacity 5 tph), producing 1.4 tph magnetite and 1 tph maghemite by-product. Three clients secured with repeat orders from two; a major off-taker requiring 5,000 tonnes monthly awaits sample product. Material sells at projected premium pricing with 350 tonnes in-stock. Layer 21 mineralogical study at Mintek progressing on schedule; initial assay results expected within 30 days. Company evaluating plant expansion given strong market demand.
retail_take: Positive catalyst: operational milestone achieved, commercial validation secured, unexpected by-product revenue stream identified, and major client pipeline indicates demand may exceed capacity—supporting near-term growth narrative.

headline: Non-Core Portfolio Exploration Results
brief: Guardian Metal Resources reports strong tungsten exploration results across expanded non-core Nevada portfolio; board assessing strategic options.
summary: Guardian Metal announced exploration results from seven non-core projects in Nevada, including newly acquired White Elephant and Cinch tungsten projects. White Elephant returned 95 ft @ 0.31% WO3, while Pilot North (near flagship Pilot Mountain) yielded 95 ft @ 0.18% WO3. Garfield project showed gold-silver-copper results including 11.2 g/t Au. The company is evaluating options for these assets while maintaining focus on flagship Pilot Mountain and Tempiute projects advancing toward production.
retail_take: Positive signal showing active exploration success and portfolio expansion, though these are non-core assets. Demonstrates technical team capability and potential value creation, but market may view as secondary to flagship project development momentum.

headline: Issue of Equity and Grant of Options
brief: ECR Minerals issues 57.7m shares for director pay and advisory fees; 25m shares to Slievemara for Paleogold deal introduction.
summary: ECR Minerals issued 57.7m ordinary shares at 0.211p per share on 13 July 2026 for director remuneration and consultant fees covering Q1 FY2026. Chairman Nick Tulloch received 14.2m shares for £30k accrued pay; directors Andrew Scott, Mike Parker, and Chris Gibbs received 6.5m, 4.9m, and 2.8m shares respectively. Separately, 25m shares at 0.26p were issued to Slievemara Consulting for introducing the Paleogold acquisition. Post-issuance, total voting rights increased to 3.6bn shares; admission to AIM expected 20 July 2026.
retail_take: Share-based remuneration is typical for junior explorers preserving cash; Slievemara payment is post-acquisition advisory fee, both routine. Dilution is material but expected given company stage and strategy.

headline: Q2 2026 Trading Update
brief: Robert Walters reports H1 2026 in line with expectations; Q2 net fees down 4% YoY but showing sequential improvement.
summary: Robert Walters' Q2 2026 net fees declined 4% year-on-year in constant currency to £69.4m, with H1 down 3% to £134.6m, demonstrating sequential improvement from full-year 2025's 14% decline. Specialist recruitment (81% of fees) fell 3%, while recruitment outsourcing grew 9%. UK performed strongly at +21%, but Europe declined 15% and Rest of World fell 20%. June showed stabilisation at +1% YoY. Headcount reduced 11% year-on-year to 2,782; net cash stood at £17.2m.
retail_take: Recruitment sector stabilisation story with green shoots in key markets offsetting weakness elsewhere; slowing decline trajectory and UK momentum likely viewed positively despite persistent European headwinds and overall fee contraction.

headline: Final Results
brief: Schroder British Opportunities Trust reports 3.0% NAV decline to 107.24p for year ended 31 March 2026, driven by multiple compression in private equity holdings.
summary: NAV fell from 81.7m to 79.2m despite strong operational performance (16.3% average EBITDA growth) across unquoted holdings. Multiple compression, particularly in technology sector, offset gains. Company completed strategic transition to 84% private equity focus (from 72%), exiting 13 quoted positions and deploying £10.7m into JMG and CSL investments. Discount to NAV narrowed from 37.1% to 34.7%. Board will put managed wind-down resolution to shareholders in Q1 2027, with continuation vote expected to pass on single-vote basis.
retail_take: Underperformance versus private equity strength signals temporary valuation headwinds rather than operational deterioration; wind-down proposal adds clarity on capital return timing. Market may view positively despite NAV decline given strategic refocus and discount narrowing.

headline: Trading Update and Strategic Review
brief: Headlam reports 22.8% H1 revenue decline and significant operating losses; undertaking strategic review to address liquidity pressures.
summary: Headlam Group's H1 2026 revenue fell 22.8% to £188.8m, driven by planned low-margin customer exits, weak markets, inventory constraints and weather impacts. The company incurred significant operating losses exceeding H1 2025. Net debt increased to £36.2m from £31.4m at year-end 2025 despite £15.3m property sale proceeds. The Board is pursuing multiple options including debt refinancing (offers already received), potential sale-leaseback of Coleshill HQ, further property sales, and wider refinancing—explicitly excluding a sale of the company.
retail_take: Material deterioration in trading metrics and mounting losses signal serious distress, though refinancing offers and strategic options suggest potential near-term liquidity relief. Market likely to react negatively on fundamentals but may await strategic review outcomes.

headline: Kabwe Drilling (“KBDD08”)
brief: Shuka Minerals completes eighth drill hole at Kabwe zinc project, identifying 3% copper mineralisation and strong zinc grades.
summary: Drill hole KBDD08 at Kabwe Zinc Mine (Zambia) intersected three mineralised zones: 18.67m at 2.05% Zn (peak 9.17%), 3.66m at 8.27% Zn & 0.93% Cu (peak 3% Cu), and 5.66m at 4.18% Zn (peak 21.55%). Speaks orebody contains 1.944MT at 12% Zn and 2% Pb; Mine Club contains 0.666MT at 11.7% Zn. Results confirm north-south continuity and expected copper oxide mineralisation. Drilling programme extended to 2,500m with two further holes planned.
retail_take: Positive signal showing exploration success at an African zinc asset with defined resources; thickening mineralisation and copper discovery supports expansion potential, though results are XRF-based pending laboratory verification.

headline: Final Results for the year ended 31 March 2026
brief: Celebrus reports strong 10.3% ARR growth to $15m but revenue down 39% due to accounting changes; restructured go-to-market ahead of FY27.
summary: FY26 saw Celebrus ARR grow 10.3% to $15m with 96.7% net revenue retention, signalling strong platform stickiness across blue-chip financial/retail customers. However, reported revenue fell 39% to $23.6m ($20.3m software revenue) due to deliberate shift to straight-line revenue recognition from April 2025, reducing near-term visibility but improving recurring quality. Company restructured commercial model into Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success functions; completed Celebrus Cloud re-platforming with AI capabilities; maintained $32.5m cash, debt-free; adjusted pre-tax profit $0.2m; increased dividend to 3.39p.
retail_take: Mixed: strong underlying metrics (ARR, retention, balance sheet) and product innovation offset disappointing new logo performance and accounting-driven revenue collapse. Market likely sees transformation story with execution risk; early FY27 pipeline signs encouraging but dividend maintenance signals confidence, not panic.

headline: Continued Positive Trading
brief: Debenhams Group reports continued positive trading with two guidance upgrades in nine months ahead of AGM.
summary: CEO Dan Finley highlighted strong GMV growth, improved margins, and reduced returns across the multi-brand portfolio. Debenhams and PLT have returned to growth and profitability. Net debt expected materially lower in current year with target below 1x Adjusted EBITDA by Feb-27. Group anticipates better EBITDA-to-operating profit conversion from H1 results onwards as transformation costs subside. Medium-term ambitions include Debenhams reaching £multi-billion GMV with £100m+ EBITDA and Karen Millen contributing material profit again.
retail_take: Market likely to view this positively: two recent guidance upgrades, concrete debt reduction targets, and resumption of profit contributions from previously struggling brands (PLT, Karen Millen) signal turnaround momentum and improving execution visibility.

headline: US$1.2m global financial services contract
brief: Brookcourt Solutions wins US$1.2m three-year email security contract with major Japanese bank.
summary: Shearwater Group's subsidiary Brookcourt Solutions has secured a US$1.2 million contract over three years with one of Japan's largest financial groups to deliver Core Email Protection technology. The solution protects against phishing, business email compromise and account takeover attempts while reducing security operations centre workloads. CEO Phil Higgins noted the win demonstrates Brookcourt's capability in enterprise cybersecurity for highly regulated organisations and adds recurring revenue.
retail_take: Positive signal of continued international traction for Brookcourt in the lucrative financial services sector, adding predictable recurring revenue; demonstrates competitive positioning in regulated markets.

headline: Full Year Trading Update
brief: Rank Group reports FY2026 underlying operating profit ahead of expectations at £76m+ on 6% NGR growth to £834.1m.
summary: Rank Group achieved 6% like-for-like NGR growth to £834.1m for the year to 30 June 2026, with full-year underlying operating profit expected to exceed £76m against consensus of £68.2m. Grosvenor venues grew 5% with gaming machine revenue up 12% in Q4, while Digital grew 8% annually despite Remote Gaming Duty increase to 40% in April 2026. The company took a £5.0m regulatory settlement provision related to historical Grosvenor Casinos compliance failings reviewed by the Gambling Commission.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: profit beat expectations, outperformance across key divisions, and management successfully navigated elevated tax headwinds while maintaining growth trajectory toward stated £100m medium-term profit target.

headline: Final Results
brief: BRCK Group reports FY26 results with 1.3% revenue growth to £645.4m and 4.5% Adjusted EBITDA growth amid construction sector headwinds.
summary: BRCK Group achieved £645.4m revenue (FY25: £637.1m) and £51.0m Adjusted EBITDA (FY25: £48.8m) for year ended 31 March 2026. Design & Install division grew 8.8% while Distribution faced headwinds. Reported profit before tax fell 46.2% to £6.3m due to £13.4m non-cash goodwill impairment from challenging market conditions. Adjusted profit before tax rose 4.9% to £38.3m. Dividend maintained at 3.51p per share. Post-period acquisition of H.S. Jackson & Son (Fencing) completed June 2026 for £20m. Net debt increased to £60.5m from £56.6m.
retail_take: Resilient operational performance masks reported losses from historical acquisition write-downs; market will focus on adjusted metrics showing 4-5% growth and maintained dividend signalling management confidence, though construction sector outlook remains challenged.

headline: H1 2026 Operations Update
brief: ACG Metals exceeds H1 2026 oxide production targets; sulphide expansion 87% complete with first copper output expected August.
summary: ACG produced 18,487 oz AuEq in H1 2026, surpassing the full-year oxide target of 17,500 oz despite a 17% production decline versus H1 2025. Gold and silver realised prices rose 64% and 142% respectively to $4,838/oz and $78.2/oz, supporting strong revenues. All-in sustaining costs increased 52% to $1,609/oz AuEq due to lower volumes and higher royalties. The Gediktepe Sulphide Expansion Project reached 87.2% completion by 30 June with first copper and zinc concentrate production expected August 2026. Net debt stands at $140 million with $60 million cash. FY2026 guidance maintained at 20-22 kt CuEq with AISC of $2.40-2.60/lb CuEq.
retail_take: Solid operational execution during transition from oxide to sulphide production; major capex largely spent; copper production launch imminent supports growth narrative and commodity upside, though near-term AISC inflation reflects mine transition phase.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Plus500 repurchased 48,120 shares on 13 July 2026 at an average price of 4,571.43p under its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Plus500 purchased 48,120 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 through Panmure Liberum at prices ranging from 4,130p to 4,898p, with a volume-weighted average price of 4,571.43p. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, 69,241,404 shares remain in issue (excluding treasury) and the company holds 45,646,973 treasury shares. Total voting rights are 69,241,404. Transactions were executed across multiple venues: XLON, CHIX, BATE, and TRQX.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution within the disclosed February 2026 programme. Demonstrates capital allocation discipline but provides no meaningful information about business fundamentals or strategy change.

headline: Klarian CLN Update
brief: Catenai seeks further extension on £699k Klarian loan repayment, originally due 1 July 2026.
summary: Catenai PLC is negotiating another extension with Klarian Ltd on a convertible loan note (CLN) originally worth £450k. The outstanding balance of £699,160 plus fees was due for repayment by 1 July 2026 under a May 2025 extension agreement, with additional monthly fees of 3% accruing. The Board remains positive about Klarian's progress, citing access to business plans, potential fundraising, and a £5m+ sales pipeline, but acknowledges no certainty of full repayment.
retail_take: Negative signal: repeated extensions on material debt repayment suggest potential collection risk. Board optimism on Klarian's prospects offers some mitigation but cannot offset mounting uncertainty over recovery timing.

headline: Change of Registered Office
brief: GEO Exploration relocates registered office to Sydney, Australia effective immediately.
summary: GEO Exploration Limited (AIM: GEO), a mineral resource and hydrocarbon exploration company, has changed its registered address to Level 7, 88 Phillip Street, Sydney, New South Wales 2000, Australia, effective 14 July 2026. The company maintains operations across Australia, Africa, and the Mediterranean, with projects including Juno and Gorge in Australia's Pilbara region, PEL0094 in Namibia, and an offshore prospect in Italy. All other company details remain unchanged.
retail_take: Administrative relocation reflecting operational focus on Australian assets; non-event for investors absent any strategic restructuring announcement.

headline: FY26 Results
brief: Watches of Switzerland reports record £1.8bn revenue, 11% growth, with US now largest market by revenue and profit.
summary: FY26 revenue reached £1.828bn (+11% reported, +13% constant currency) with Adjusted EBIT of £155m (+6% constant currency). US revenue grew 24% in constant currency to £927m, now exceeding 50% of group revenue and profit, while UK grew 5% to £901m. Statutory PBT jumped 76% to £133m. Free cash flow surged to £162m (80% conversion) with net debt reduced to £57m. FY27 guidance targets 5-10% revenue growth at constant currency with 40-80bps Adjusted EBIT margin expansion.
retail_take: Strong execution and margin recovery guidance ahead likely appeals to growth-focused investors; US momentum and strategic investments signal confidence, though UK consumer softness and tariff headwinds remain near-term concerns.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 17,609 shares at 892.50p-908.50p on 13 July under its ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Gamma Communications purchased 17,609 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at prices ranging from 892.50p to 908.50p, with a weighted average price of 903.51p, via Investec Bank. The shares will be cancelled. Cumulative purchases under the programme announced 13 January 2026 total 2,667,373 shares. Post-settlement, 91,096,978 shares remain in issue with 1,524,392 held in treasury, giving 89,572,586 voting shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution during offer period; incremental capital allocation signal but no material market catalyst given programme's earlier announcement and ongoing takeover bid context.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Airtel Africa repurchased 1.86m shares (7-10 July) at 316.8-327.2p average; 12.86m cumulative since May buyback launch.
summary: During 6-10 July 2026, Airtel Africa purchased 1,855,779 ordinary shares from Barclays Capital Securities at volume-weighted average prices ranging from 321.46p to 338.13p daily. Total shares purchased since programme inception (22 May) reached 12,864,569 at 339.40p VWAP. All repurchased shares will be cancelled. Purchases executed across London Stock Exchange and various European trading venues.
retail_take: Routine capital management signalling confidence in valuation and capital efficiency; buybacks typically neutral-to-mildly positive absent other catalysts, reducing share count but immaterial to near-term sentiment.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
