summary: GlobalData PLC purchased 99,475 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 through Investec Bank plc at prices ranging from 74.90p to 77.00p, with a weighted average price of 75.9234p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing total voting rights to 746,095,973. This represents the first tranche disclosed under the buyback programme that commenced 23 June 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; demonstrates capital allocation discipline but provides limited market insight. Non-event unless buyback scale or price trajectory proves materially different from guidance.

headline: Agreement with PyWave Network Technology Limited
brief: RC365 partners with Canadian PyWave for cross-border payments and virtual banking to support RC3.0 launch.
summary: RC365's subsidiary RCPAY signed a 24-month agreement with PyWave Network Technology Limited to provide cross-border and local payment services. Key features include banking virtual account services with umbrella structure for sub-accounts, international remittance capabilities, and local/overseas payment collection with phased rollout prioritising African markets. Agreement includes API integration, comprehensive AML/CTF compliance frameworks, and auto-renews for 12-month periods unless terminated.
retail_take: Strategic partnership likely viewed positively as it expands RC365's payment infrastructure and geographic reach into high-growth African markets, supporting commercial development and merchant acquisition plans.

headline: Completion of Belgium property sale
brief: Victoria PLC completes €34.4m Belgian distribution centre sale-leaseback, funding Turkey manufacturing relocation.
summary: Victoria PLC has completed the €34.4 million sale and leaseback of its Belgian distribution centre to Avantage Property Holding BV. The asset had a net book value of €5.6m as of 31 January 2026, generating a significant gain. Tax losses will mitigate capital gains tax. Proceeds, combined with two additional surplus property disposals in process, will fully fund exceptional costs and capital expenditure for relocating the majority of Balta Rug's production to Turkey, expected complete Q3 FY2027. The Belgian centre remains the key European distribution hub.
retail_take: Positive signal: company effectively funding substantial Turkey relocation strategy through asset sales without additional capital raise, while maintaining operational distribution capacity and utilizing tax losses. De-risks execution of strategic manufacturing shift.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: PCFT H1 results show 4.8% NAV total return, outperforming 3.1% benchmark. New 4% annual dividend policy introduced.
summary: Polar Capital Global Financials Trust delivered 4.8% NAV total return in six months to 31 May 2026, outperforming its MSCI ACWI Financials benchmark (3.1%). Share price total return was 5.7% with discount narrowing to 4.3%. Total net assets £372m. EPS of 10.34p (revenue 3.37p, capital 6.97p). Board adopted enhanced dividend policy targeting ~4% annual NAV payout quarterly. During period, 6.78m shares (4.1% of capital) were repurchased. Net gearing at 1.3% net cash position as of 9 July 2026.
retail_take: Modest outperformance against benchmark with improved dividend policy should appeal to income-focused investors, though wider equity markets significantly outpaced financials in H1 2026, limiting relative appeal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited (HVPE), a FTSE 250 investment company, purchased 7,000 of its own ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at an average price of £33.6088 per share for cancellation, exercising authority granted at its July 2025 AGM. Following this transaction, the Company now has 69,647,352 shares in issue. The share count figure serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution reducing share count; typical capital management activity that slightly enhances per-share metrics but signals no material strategic change or distress.

headline: IQE plc: $14m AI & datacentre production order
brief: IQE secures $14m multi-year production order from strategic customer for AI and datacentre applications.
summary: IQE plc has won a $14 million multi-year production order from an unnamed strategic global technology customer for AI and datacentre markets. Manufacturing will occur at IQE's Newport foundry, supporting high-performance storage technologies. The company is also developing next-generation technologies with the customer across multiple data lifecycle stages, leveraging its epitaxy portfolio including indium phosphide optical communications, silicon photonics and gallium arsenide VCSEL-based datacom applications.
retail_take: Concrete revenue win in high-growth AI/datacentre segment validates IQE's market positioning and foundry capacity, though the unnamed customer and 'multi-year' timeframe lack specificity for near-term impact assessment.

headline: Purchase of Own Shares

summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchased 20,000 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at 157.41 pence per share on AIM, bringing cumulative buyback total to 5,203,100 shares held in treasury. Following this purchase, total issued share capital comprises 137,125,806 shares with 131,922,706 carrying voting rights after treasury holdings. The buyback programme was announced on 4 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under previously announced programme; modest daily purchase activity typical of structured repurchase plans with no immediate market implications.

retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under pre-announced programme; modest scale suggests measured capital management rather than aggressive consolidation signal.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchased 500,000 shares at 134.52p average price on 13 July 2026.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 500,000 ordinary shares through Investec Bank plc on 13 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 134.5224p (range 133.20p-134.60p). retail_take: Share buyback suggests management views stock as undervalued; supports earnings per share accretion. Routine capital management activity that typically signals management confidence, though scale is modest relative to issued shares.

retail_take: Routine share buyback for cancellation; modest volume suggesting ongoing capital management, typically read as neutral or mildly supportive of EPS.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IWG repurchased 156,909 shares on 13 July at average price of £1.9257, bringing total programme purchases to 39.4m shares since December 2025.
summary: International Workplace Group plc continued its share buyback programme authorised at its May 2026 AGM. On 13 July 2026, it purchased 156,909 shares at a volume-weighted average price of £1.9257 across five venues (LSE, CBOE CXE, CBOE BXE, Turquoise, Aquis). Since programme launch on 31 December 2025, IWG has repurchased 39.39 million shares. Post-purchase, 954.9 million shares remain in issue, excluding treasury holdings. Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; buybacks support EPS accretion and signal management confidence in valuation, but this single-day trade is a non-event for investors absent broader strategic context.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: Getech reports strong H1 FY26 with 15% revenue growth to £2.4m and return to adjusted EBITDA profitability of £0.2m.
summary: GETECH (AIM: GTC) achieved H1 FY26 revenue of £2.4m (up 15% from £2.1m) with adjusted EBITDA of £0.2m versus prior-year loss of £0.1m, reflecting 18-month strategic refocus and 20% cost reduction. Orderbook stood at £4.0m with £1.6m expected in H2. Cash increased to £0.6m from £0.2m. Annual recurring revenue stable at £2.8m. Management noted strengthened trading momentum, expanded new business pipeline, and positive demand across Oil & Gas, Mining, and Natural Hydrogen sectors, positioning the company to meet FY26 market expectations.
retail_take: Positive momentum: return to profitability, revenue growth, improved cash position, and strong pipeline suggest the turnaround strategy is working, though modest absolute profit and small cash balance warrant monitoring of execution.

headline: Q3 Trading Update
brief: IntegraFin Q3 update shows strong momentum with £85.5bn FUD, up 23% YoY, and £1bn net inflows.
summary: IntegraFin's Transact platform reached £85.5bn funds under direction at Q3 end, up 23% year-on-year from £69.5bn. Net inflows of £1.0bn in Q3 FY26 marked the sixth consecutive quarter above £1bn, with gross inflows of £2.6bn up 5% YoY. Average daily FUD hit a record £82.8bn, up 24% YoY. The company reiterated cost guidance expecting underlying administrative expense growth to slow to circa 3% in FY26 and FY27, with greater savings materializing in H2.
retail_take: Strong organic growth, record platform scale, and cost management initiatives likely support positive investor sentiment; consistent net inflows and market recovery driving revenue momentum expected to translate to earnings leverage.

retail_take: Routine share buyback under existing shareholder mandate; modest scale and standard treasury holding practice suggest capital management rather than strategic signal.

headline: AGM Statement
brief: CelLBxHealth confirms FY2026 revenue guidance of £2.1m (50% growth) at AGM; CEO search underway.
summary: CelLBxHealth held its AGM on 14 July 2026 with Executive Chairman Jan Groen reaffirming the company remains on track to deliver FY2026 revenues of at least £2.1 million, representing 50% growth on FY2025. Groen stated nothing fundamental has changed in the core business and commercial opportunities remain significant. The board has begun recruiting a new Chief Executive Officer but expects no trading impact given sufficient senior leadership capacity.
retail_take: Steady-state guidance reconfirmation with CEO transition ongoing; demonstrates management continuity but leadership gap may concern some investors until appointment announced.

headline: APTA Continues to Deliver
brief: Orosur's APTA prospect in Colombia delivers strong gold results from second hole in new drill phase.
summary: Hole MAP107 at the APTA prospect intersected 135.5m @ 1.91g/t Au, including 49m @ 4.06g/t Au and nested higher-grade zones up to 12.73g/t Au. The hole ended in mineralisation. Results confirm gold mineralisation exists at shallower levels than previous drilling, suggesting a larger deposit than historically thought. The company is drilling eastward from a new location to test additional shallow mineralisation while geological modelling may support a future mineral resource estimate.
retail_take: Strong drill results at a maturing prospect with demonstrated continuity and higher-grade zones validate new geological concepts, likely supporting positive momentum for junior explorer with full funding backing.

headline: AGM Trading Update
brief: British Land reports strong Q1 leasing momentum across campuses and retail parks, reiterates FY27 EPS guidance of at least 30.5p.
summary: British Land completed 567,000 sq ft of leasing in Q1 FY27, 4.8% ahead of ERV and 8.7% ahead of previous rents, with a further 1.1m sq ft under offer. Campuses saw 264,000 sq ft completed and 407,000 sq ft under offer; Broadgate Tower reached 63% let/under offer; One Triton Square achieved 99% occupancy. Retail parks delivered 157,000 sq ft with deals 13.7% ahead of passing rents. The company recycled £83m of assets and reiterates FY27 Underlying EPS guidance of at least 30.5p, supported by like-for-like net rental growth at the top end of its 3-5% target range.
retail_take: Strong operational performance with leasing ahead of rents and robust pipeline supports reiterated earnings guidance; market likely views positively as momentum validates exposure to London campuses and retail parks, though cautious given property sector headwinds.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Glanbia PLC purchased 25,441 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 on Euronext Dublin via J&E Davy at prices ranging from €23.98 to €24.06 per share, with a volume-weighted average price of €24.0329. These shares will be cancelled. The purchase forms part of the company's announced €50 million share buyback programme that commenced 25 February 2026 and runs through 30 September 2026. Post-cancellation, Glanbia will have 238,975,966 ordinary shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine capital management execution within announced buyback programme parameters. Share buybacks typically indicate management confidence in valuation but carry no immediate operational significance for investors.

headline: Issue of Equity and TVR
brief: Rockwood Strategic issues 50,000 shares at 313.92p per share via blocklisting facility.
summary: Rockwood Strategic PLC issued 50,000 ordinary shares of 5p each at 313.92p per share on 15 July 2026 via its blocklisting facility, raising approximately £156.96m in cash. Post-issuance, the company has 60,643,760 ordinary shares in issue with no treasury holdings. This figure establishes the voting rights denominator for shareholder disclosure notifications under FCA rules.
retail_take: Routine capital raise announcement with standard post-issuance voting rights notification; no material strategic insight provided, likely viewed as administrative disclosure.

headline: Corporate Strategy
brief: Sunrise Resources refocuses strategy on precious and base metals exploration, deprioritising industrial minerals to unlock value.
summary: Following strategic review, Sunrise Resources will prioritise exploration of wholly owned precious and base metals assets in Nevada and Western Australia, whilst maintaining industrial minerals projects at minimal cost. Portfolio includes high-quality projects: Clayton (epithermal silver-gold with 7.9m @ 303 g/t Ag), Reese Ridge (carbonate replacement zinc-lead-silver), Newark (Carlin-style gold), and Bakers (shear-hosted gold, 2m @ 14.36 g/t Au). Board plans share consolidation and will appoint additional mining industry director. Backdrop: silver up 67%, gold 25%, copper and zinc up 30% over past 12 months; US government support for domestic minerals exploration.
retail_take: Strategy shift aligns with strong commodity prices and investor appetite for exploration plays; consolidation and board strengthening suggest preparation for growth, though execution risk remains on exploration results and funding.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Currys PLC purchased 1,463,020 ordinary shares for cancellation between 7-13 July 2026 via Panmure Liberum. Prices ranged from 154.90p to 160.00p, with weekly averages between 157.28p-157.48p across venues (XLON, CHIX, BATE, TRQX). Following cancellation, issued shares fall to 1,093,205,670. This continues the £100m buyback programme announced 2 July 2026, authorised at the September 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution reducing share count; modest EPS accretion but non-catalytic. Market likely treats as neutral housekeeping unless broader capital allocation strategy shifts.

retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and provide modest EPS accretion through share count reduction; this routine repurchase is likely viewed as capital allocation rather than a material market event.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Babcock International repurchased 67,455 ordinary shares (60p par) on 13 July 2026 via Jefferies at volume-weighted average price of £10.0377, with trades ranging 9.942p to 10.160p across multiple venues. Since 1 July, the company has acquired 654,657 shares for £6.6m. Post-transaction, Babcock holds 13.2m treasury shares and has 492.4m shares in issue. Shares purchased are retained in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback signalling capital allocation and potential EPS accretion; consistent with prior July activity but immaterial to near-term outlook without context on buyback programme limits.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Feedback PLC reports flat revenue of £0.8m but improved cash position of £2.7m; awaiting NHS national contract decision by year-end.
summary: FY26 revenue flat at c.£0.8m (FY25: £0.9m) with lower EBITDA losses than expected due to cost controls. Cash balances improved to £2.7m with runway through mid-2027. Company pursuing significant national NHS contract for Bleepa technology; decision expected around calendar year-end following pilot success at Queen Victoria Hospital. New integrations with NHS e-Referral Service (tenfold increase in GP referrals), Patients Know Best, and Evolve EMR completed. Bleepa v1.7 released with enhanced features. Sussex deployment shows 100% breathlessness patient diversion from outpatient appointments; insomnia pathway successfully rolled out across Kent, Surrey, Sussex.
retail_take: Market likely cautious; while improved cash position and operational metrics are positive, actual revenue remains flat and company remains pre-profitability. Material upside depends entirely on uncertain NHS contract decision outside company control—makes this a speculative holding despite operational progress.

headline: H1 and Q2 2026 Trading Update
brief: DP Poland reports strong H1 2026 with 17.5% system sales growth and network expansion to 139 Domino's stores.
summary: DP Poland delivered H1 2026 group system sales of £34.8m, up 17.5% year-on-year on constant currency (20.6% reported), with system orders rising 11.5% to 2.7m. The Domino's network expanded to 139 stores (Poland 132, Croatia 7), with franchisee-owned locations increasing to 53 (38% of estate) from 15% year-ago. Pizzeria 105 conversions continued with five additional stores converted; 18 total converted stores showed 17.7% sales growth post-conversion. Like-for-like growth moderated in Q2 against tough comparatives, though Poland delivered 6.2% LFL sales growth for H1. The board remains confident in full-year outlook, anticipating continued double-digit system sales growth in H2 2026.
retail_take: Solid execution on franchise transition and network expansion strategy with double-digit growth, though Q2 like-for-like moderation against strong comparatives suggests momentum cooling; margin expansion ambitions depend on conversion acceleration and commissary consolidation payoff.

headline: Civil Projects Division Secures £6.1m of Contracts
brief: Hercules' Civil Projects division secures £6.1m in new water sector contracts during Q2/Q3 FY2026.
summary: Hercules plc's Civil Projects division has won approximately £6.1 million in contract awards during Q2 and Q3 of FY2026, bringing nine-month total awards to over £15.7 million. Contracts worth c.£4.5 million were awarded in the Thames Water region and c.£1.6 million in the Anglian Water region, forming part of the UK's £104 billion AMP8 regulatory investment programme (2025-2030). The majority of work will be delivered during FY2026, demonstrating growing demand for the division's capabilities; the division generated £13.5 million revenue in FY2025.
retail_take: Positive read: strong contract pipeline growth, strategic positioning in lucrative long-term water infrastructure spending, and demonstrated client confidence support division expansion trajectory.

headline: Molaoi Drilling Update
brief: Rockfire's Molaoi zinc drilling continues successfully, infilling another 50m panel with good grades ahead of feasibility study.
summary: Hole HMO-018 returned strong zinc grades including 3.13m @ 6.0% Zn with 52.7g/t silver and 18.2g/t germanium at 211.38m depth. Results support upgrading Inferred Resources to Indicated status for the 100%-owned Greek deposit, which currently holds 15.0 million tonnes @ 7.26% Zn. The company is fast-tracking feasibility studies including geotechnical drilling and permitting work, expected to commence this year and take ~4 months. A drilling rig is scheduled to arrive Athens in late September/early October.
retail_take: Steady progress on resource definition with consistent mineralization supporting project advancement, but announcement lacks major new discovery or material near-term production catalysts.

headline: Iron Mineralisation Confirmed at Pilbara Projects
brief: Alien Metals confirms iron mineralisation at Pilbara projects adjacent to major producers' deposits.
summary: Independent field reconnaissance by ERM Australia confirmed hematite canga and martite-goethite mineralisation at Vivash (E47/3071) and Brockman (E47/3953) projects in Western Australia's Pilbara region. Vivash sits adjacent to Fortescue's Vivash Southwest deposit (28Mt @ 58.8% Fe), while Brockman is positioned near BHP's Deposit 20 and FMG's Farquhar East. Q3 2026 field programs planned for Hancock Project targeting Weeli Wolli and Boolgeeda Iron Formations with significant unexplored strike lengths.
retail_take: Early-stage exploration validation in proven mining district provides directional support but requires further drilling to establish resources; positive signal though typical speculative junior activity.

headline: Full Year Results and Trading Update
brief: Sosandar reports 14% revenue growth to £42.3m with improved 64% gross margins and returned to profitability in FY26.
summary: Women's fashion retailer Sosandar delivered FY26 revenue of £42.3m (up 14% YoY) with gross margin expanding to 64.0% from 62.1%, driving adjusted PBT to £0.4m. Own-website revenue grew 24% YoY; underlying business (excluding loss-making stores) generated £1.3m adjusted PBT. Net cash position strengthened to £8.4m despite £1.8m shareholder buybacks. Q1 FY27 trading continued momentum with 22% net revenue growth to £11.6m and further margin expansion to 65.2%, with management reiterating market expectations.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: demonstrated profitable growth delivery after strategic repositioning, proven multi-channel model, strong cash generation, and confident forward guidance supported by Q1 momentum.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

Post-cancellation, the company will have 173,818,349 voting ordinary shares outstanding with 42,041 treasury shares (0.02% of voting rights). headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Pershing Square Holdings repurchased 37,675 shares on 13 July 2026 at average price of $52.09 per share.
summary: PSH purchased 37,675 public shares through Jefferies on 13 July 2026: 26,385 shares on LSE (PSH ticker) at average 3,894 pence/$52.09 and 11,290 shares (PSHD ticker) at $52.07. NAV per share was $77.24/£57.81 as of 7 July. Post-buyback, 173.9m shares remain outstanding with 37.0m treasury shares held. The buyback is accretive at NAV discount pricing.
retail_take: Share buyback at discount to NAV is modestly positive, indicating management confidence and capital efficiency, though represents routine treasury management for a closed-ended fund.

headline: Annual Financial Report
brief: MIGO Opportunities Trust reports 20.6% NAV return for year ended 30 April 2026, delivering strong performance through concentrated portfolio of discounted investment trusts.
summary: MIGO's NAV per share rose 20.6% to 413.1p (share price +21.9% to 398.5p) for the year to 30 April 2026, outperforming its SONIA+2% benchmark (6.1%). Net assets grew to £70.1m. The trust completed a strategic transition to a more concentrated portfolio with 10-15 core holdings, reducing listed equity exposure from 28% to 5%. Management fee reduced to 35bps with performance fee introduced; NAV discount narrowed to 3.5%. First dividend since 2004 of 2.0p per share approved, reflecting higher-yielding investments. Key contributors included Baker Steel Resources Trust (+641bps) and Georgia Capital (+425bps).
retail_take: Strong absolute returns and successful portfolio transition position MIGO as a focused activist play on investment trust discounts; narrowing discount and improved fee alignment with shareholders are positive signals, though reliance on corporate catalysts carries execution risk.

headline: SATO Corporation Half-Year Report 1 January–30 June 2026: SATO’s steady development continued
Transaction in Own Shares
brief: TotalEnergies repurchased 1.51m shares for €103m during July 6-10, 2026, at average price of €68.20.
summary: TotalEnergies SE executed its authorized share buyback program, purchasing 1,510,048 shares across five trading days (July 6-10, 2026) for a total of €102,987,680.64. The weighted average purchase price was €68.20 per share, with volumes ranging from 158,950 to 190,448 shares daily. Shares were bought across multiple venues including Euronext Paris (primary market), Cboe Europe, Turquoise, and Aquis Exchange.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under shareholder authorization; typically neutral signal suggesting management views stock as fairly valued and aims to manage capital structure and EPS accretion.

headline: NBPE Announces Transaction in Own Shares
brief: NBPE bought back 12,101 Class A shares at £14.66-£14.70 on 13 July, reducing outstanding shares to 40.9m.
summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited repurchased 12,101 Class A shares on London Stock Exchange on 13 July 2026 at prices between £14.66 and £14.70 per share, under shareholder-approved general authority. All repurchased shares will be cancelled, reducing outstanding Class A shares from approximately 40.9 million to 40.889 million. The company maintains 3.15 million Class A shares in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically support earnings per share but signal neither bullish nor bearish sentiment on own valuation at current prices.

headline: Capita to deliver training for Army Collective Training System contract as part of Omnia Training consortium
brief: Capita awarded £54m share of £2bn, 15-year British Army training contract via Omnia Training consortium.
summary: Capita will deliver training services for the British Army's Collective Training System (ACTS) as part of a consortium led by Raytheon UK, alongside Cervus, Rheinmetall UK and Skyral. The 15-year contract is valued at £2 billion overall, with Capita's IFRS 15 order book allocation of £54 million. Capita's responsibilities include strategic training needs analysis, training scheduling, and evaluation of training effectiveness.
retail_take: Long-term government contract win provides revenue visibility and demonstrates Capita's capability in defence sector, though £54m over 15 years (£3.6m annually) is modest relative to group scale.

summary: BlackRock Frontiers Investment Trust plc disclosed voting rights as at 14 July 2026, with issued share capital of 164,891,520 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote per share. The company holds 76,931,281 shares in treasury. Shareholders must use 164,891,520 as the denominator for calculating notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules 5.6.1.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights denominator; establishes threshold baseline for shareholder notification requirements; no material implications for investors.

headline: Chair Succession
brief: Sir Ian Cheshire appointed Chair of Anglian Water, succeeding Dr Ros Rivaz effective 14 July 2026.
summary: Dr Ros Rivaz has stepped down as Chair of Anglian Water Group and Anglian Water Services following a planned transition. Sir Ian Cheshire, who brings extensive FTSE 100 board experience and previous chair roles at Landsec and Channel 4, takes over effective 14 July 2026. The transition is timed to ensure leadership continuity as Anglian Water progresses its £11bn investment programme and develops two new reservoirs (Fens and Lincs) from planning into construction phases.
retail_take: Routine governance succession with experienced replacement; continuity messaging and timing around major capital projects likely viewed as well-managed by the market with no strategy changes.

summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc notified the market of its share capital composition as at 14 July 2026, comprising 60,960,138 Ordinary Shares in issue, each carrying one vote per share. An additional 34,401,167 Ordinary Shares are held in treasury. The company has designated 60,960,138 as the denominator for shareholder calculations regarding notification thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1.

headline: Chair Succession

Sir Ian Cheshire, who brings extensive FTSE 100 board experience and previous Chair roles, assumes the position effective 14 July 2026. The succession occurs as Anglian Water progresses a £11bn+ investment programme and develops two major new reservoirs (Fens and Lincs), with no change to company strategy or priorities resulting from this transition.
retail_take: Orderly succession with experienced external chair suggests continuity and strong governance; likely viewed positively given Cheshire's regulatory credentials and sector experience, though no material strategic changes announced.

headline: Chair Succession
brief: Anglian Water appoints Sir Ian Cheshire as Chair, succeeding Dr Ros Rivaz effective 14 July 2026.
summary: Dr Ros Rivaz stepped down as Chair of Anglian Water Group and Anglian Water Services following a planned transition. Sir Ian Cheshire, who brings extensive FTSE 100 governance experience and currently chairs Landsec and Spire Healthcare, replaces her effective 14 July 2026. The transition ensures continuity during critical phases including two reservoir developments (Fens and Lincs) and an £11bn investment programme. No strategy changes result from this succession.
retail_take: Planned leadership transition with experienced operator at helm during major capital projects; orderly succession reduces execution risk. Market likely views positively given Cheshire's track record in transformation and sustainability.

headline: Monthly Fund Factsheet & Commentary - June 2026
brief: TwentyFour Income Fund publishes June 2026 factsheet showing 10.12% current yield and 2.26% premium to NAV.
summary: TFIF, a FTSE 250 listed closed-ended investment company, released its June 2026 factsheet with NAV per share at 107.37p and share price at 109.80p. Current mark-to-market yield stands at 10.12% with forward yield to maturity of 10.00%. Year-to-date NAV performance was 2.20%, with 1-year return of 6.95%. Portfolio concentrated in asset-backed securities (47% RMBS, 31% CLO, 8% SRT), primarily UK and European exposure. Market commentary highlights strong ABS technicals, €92bn H1 supply ahead 20% year-on-year, and robust institutional demand despite geopolitical tensions.
retail_take: Strong fund performance with double-digit yields in a higher-for-longer rate environment supports the income investment case; premium to NAV and robust technicals suggest market confidence, though macro headwinds flagged warrant monitoring.

headline: Share Repurchase Program - Weekly Report
brief: Sunbelt Rentals repurchased 43,000 shares (July 6-10) under its $1.5bn program at weighted average $70.95.
summary: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings executed 43,000 share repurchases during the week of July 6-10, 2026, across NYSE at varying prices: July 6 at $72.26, July 7 at $69.71, July 8 at $68.95, July 9 at $72.05, and July 10 at $74.21. Treasury holdings now total 4,329,501 shares; shares outstanding reduced to 409,634,584. The repurchase program totals $1.5 billion.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation activity demonstrating disciplined shareholder return execution. Share buybacks at modest scale weekly indicate steady program progression; market-neutral absent broader context.

company: TwentyFour Select Monthly Income Fund Limited
headline: Monthly Factsheet & Commentary June 2026
brief: TwentyFour Select Monthly Income Fund publishes June 2026 factsheet showing solid monthly performance amid geopolitical volatility.
summary: SMIF generated 0.94% return in June with Additional Tier 1 securities as top performer. The fund holds £306.32mn in market capitalisation across 356.6mn shares, trading at 85.90p with NAV of 84.51p (1.64% premium). Current yield stands at 8.46% with monthly distributions of 0.50-0.75p. Portfolio is heavily weighted to European banks (61.47%), with 42.1% BBB-rated securities. One-year NAV return was 8.30%; three-year annualised return 14.41%.
retail_take: Routine monthly disclosure showing resilient credit fund performance in uncertain macro environment. Strong yield and stable pricing support the monthly income mandate, though macro headwinds (sticky inflation, geopolitical risks) persist. Non-event for most investors unless reassessment of illiquid credit exposure is warranted.

headline: Transactions by Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities
brief: Eight Sunbelt Rentals directors settled deferred stock units on 8-10 July 2026 at $76.53/unit under 2017 Deferred Bonus Plan.
summary: Eight senior executives at Sunbelt Rentals Holdings executed cash settlements of deferred stock units between 8-10 July 2026. Brendan Horgan (CEO) settled 12,890 units; Alex Pease (CFO) 4,032 units; Lynne Fuller-Andrews (General Counsel) 3,370 units; John Washburn (COO) 10,464 units across three days at prices ranging $70.00-$76.53; Kyle Horgan (EVP Specialty) 3,115 units; Brad Lull (EVP Strategy) 3,387 units; Barbara Clark (Chief Accounting Officer) 3,166 units. All settlements at or near $76.53 closing price on 30 April 2026, with no new shares issued.
retail_take: Routine stock compensation vesting with mixed signals—senior executives realised deferred bonus plan units, but one director (Washburn) made open market sales below settlement price, potentially signalling caution on valuation.

headline: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings, Inc. files Current Report on Form 8-K
brief: Sunbelt Rentals expands board from nine to ten directors; appoints Ekta Singh-Bushell effective August 1, 2026.
summary: Sunbelt Rentals Holdings Inc. (NYSE: SUNB; LSE: SUNB) filed a Form 8-K on July 13, 2026, announcing that its Board of Directors has increased from nine to ten members effective August 1, 2026. Ms. Ekta Singh-Bushell was elected to fill the vacancy and appointed to the Audit Committee. The announcement was made via SEC filing and press release on the company's investor relations website.
retail_take: Routine governance update; board expansion and committee appointment signal normal corporate administration with no material strategic implications for investors.
